Description: The identifier of the analysis from which the results were calculated or the data was used in.

The date when the data snapshot details are relevant. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
Marked as conditional as this is a required input if data is provided in the category (such as instrumentCashFlow).
For data from CreditLens or RiskAnalyst, this is the date the data is extracted.
For the scorecard model, this is the rating assesment date.

Custom attribute for asset class.
Contains the FFIEC Code (index 55) value from the the Interagency Loan Data (ILD) file.

The loss given default as a percentage of the exposure. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are equal to or greater than 0. Valid values for the input LGD into the GCorr model are between 0 and 1.

Identifier for the scenario. Its value should not include blank spaces.
For inputs, the scenario describes the assumptions used to produce the inputs. If there are no assumptions, this field should be filled with 0. 0 will be used to retrieve model and market data inputs when no scenarios are selected in scenario settings. For outputs, it is the scenario used to produce the results. In addition, Moody's Analytics uses the value Summary for the weighted average results.

The time period for which the value is relevant. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 200 or less. The term should have a maximum of 4 digit precision and rounded up. This guidance is primarily for the term for the EAD converted from cash flows.

ImpairmentStudio calculates the vintage of the instrument from the origination date. Its format is as yyyyQ#, where # is 1, 2, 3, or 4. For the MA Expected Consumer Credit Loss models, vintages prior to 2005 does not adhere to the ImpairmentStudio calculated vintage. For vintages prior to 2005, you must import these vintage values: 1990Q3 for instruments originated prior to 1990, 1995Q3 for origination between 1990 to 1995, and the year + Q3 for 1996 to 2004 (i.e. 1996Q3, 2004Q3).

An identifier to uniquely identify each instrument. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument. For reinsurance receivable, this value is the treaty identifier. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information.

The instrument identifier that links the line of credit and its draws. If the instrument is the Line of Credit, this value is the same as its instrument identifier. This is marked as conditional as this is required for line of credit or loans drawn from a line of credit.

The group to which the loan and property belongs. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) when there is more than one loan and one property in the group.

The group to which the loan and property belongs. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) when there is more than one loan and one property in the group.
The description of the group to which the loan and property belongs.
The identifier from the loan accounting system.

An identifier to uniquely identify each entity. Each entity must have one entity identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the entity identifier for any other entity. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information.
The Moody's Analytics counterparty identifier.

The obligated party (bond issuer, borrower, debtor, insurer, etc.) who has an obligation to repay a debt, make a payment, do something, or to refrain from doing something, under the terms of an agreement. For reinsurance receivable, this value is the reinsurer or assuming entity's identifier. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information. Marked as conditional as this input is required for reinsurance receivables and the value is the reinsurer or assuming entity's identifier.

Type that describes the entity. Valid values include Private Firm, Public Firm, Sovereign, Municipality, Supranational, and SME. Unknown is used when the entity type is not known.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for RiskCalc LGD and to create the scope for determining which Moody's analytics models to use, such as the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, MA Sovereign TTC to PIT PD Converter, and Muni Loss Forecast Model.
The description.

Custom attribute for asset class.
Contains the Type (index 54) value from the the Interagency Loan Data (ILD) file.

Assesses a company's management practices with regards to opportunities associated with the Access to Finance Key Issue. Inferred based on management proxies. Expressed as a 0 to 10 score, where 10 indicates best management practices.

Custom attribute for asset class.
Contains the Collateral Code (index 32) value from the the Interagency Loan Data (ILD) file.
The date of default. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Provide this value if the borrower has defaulted.

Indicates the type of default. Valid values are 180DaysPastDue, 270DaysPastDue, 90DaysPastDue, 90DaysTimesPastDue, AdministratorAppointed, Bailout, Bankruptcy, BankruptcyChapter10, BankruptcyChapter11, BankruptcyChapter7, BankruptcyChapter9, BankruptcyLiquidation, ChargedOff, CollateralPropertyAttached, CompositionProceeding, DaysPastDue, DaysPastDuePassRating, Dissolution, Doubtful, Extinction, FacilitiesCalled, Insolvency, Liquidation, LoanLossProvision, Loss, Moratorium, NonaccrualLoan, OLEM, Receivership, Reorganization, RestructuredDebt, SoftDefault, SpecificLloanLossReserveCreated, Substandard, Substandard30DPD, Substandard60DPD, Unknown, UnlikelyToPay, VoluntaryArrangement, VoluntaryLiquidation, and WindingUp.
Marked as conditional as this is a required input for RiskCalc LGD to determine if the borrower is in bankruptcy.

The state or province where the borrower is located. Valid values are 2 letter state abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apb.htm or 2 letter province abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apa_005.htm.
The zip code where the borrower is located.

The US regions recognized by Moody's models. Values are: Combined, Midwest, Northeast, South, and West. See the list of values for the mapping between the US State and the region. This is for the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) 2017 primary industry for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity.
When marked as conditional, this a required input to lookup the Moody's sector (i.e. GCorr sector) for use in models such as the GCorr, MA CI Proxy PD/LGD and Loss Rate models, MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, and RiskCalc LGD.

The Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) is a four-digit code for the primary industry for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input to lookup the Moody's sector (i.e. GCorr sector).

The sectors for municipalities. Valid values are: Airports, Healthcare, Higher Education, Housing, Local Governments, Not-For-Profit, Public Power, Ports, Public Power, States, Toll Facilities, and Water & Sewer. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for the MA Muni Loss Forecast model when the CUSIP is not provided.

The industry or sector recognized by Moody's models. Valid values are Agriculture, ArtsAndEntertainment, Auto, Business_Products, Business_Services, Communication, Construction, Consumer_Products, Finance, Education, Health_Care, HiTech, Manufacturing, Mining, Other, Other_Services, Services, Trade, Transportation, Unassigned, and Utilities.
The values listed support the following models: MA CI Loss Rate, MA CI Proxy PD/LGD, and MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter. For each model, it is a required input and each model uses a subset of the values listed above. The ImpairmentStudio Data Integration Guide provides the sector values that each model supports.
If this field is set to a Moody's industry sector value that is not supported by the specific Moody's model, the model use the value Unasssigned.

The Moody's LGD sector. Valid values are: AerospaceMeasure, BanksAndSL, CableTVPrintingPublishing, ConsumerGoodsAndDurables, Equipment, General, HighTech, MaterialsExtraction, Medical, Municipals, OtherFinancials, REIT, Sovereign, Transportation, Unknown, and Utilities.
This is required for the RiskCalc LGD model.

Total sales of the borrower. The amount entered in instrument or entity reference must be in USD. The amount in the financial statement is in the currency for the financial statement. When marked as conditional, the value in instrument reference is a required input to lookup the GCorr R-squared parameters for private non financial firms.

The total assets of the borrower which is the sum of the current assets, plus net property, plant, and equipment, plus other non-current assets (including intangible assets, deferred items, and investments and advances). The amount entered in instrument or entity reference must be in USD. The amount in the financial statement is in the currency for the financial statement. The amount in instrument reference is a required input for the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models as it is used to calculate the Total Assets Size Bucket. When marked as conditional, the amount in instrument reference is a required input to lookup the GCorr R-squared parameters for private financial firms.
The bucket in which the total assets fall in. Valid values are: Combined, A: <1M USD, B: 1-5M USD, C: 5-10M USD, D: 10-50M USD, and E: >50M USD. This is for the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD model.

Indicates the intent to sell. TRUE indicates the intention to sell as well as the likelihood that the security will be required to be sold before recovery. For the current expected loss calculation (CECL), this is required to be set to TRUE if there is an intent to sell. Default is FALSE.
Indicate accrual or non-accrual assets. Valid values are: Accrual assets and Non-accrual assets. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.

The compounding frequency used to calculate the fair value. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Discount and Monthly.
For interest rate input, this is marked as conditional as this is a required input if the interest rate curve form is SPOT. If no value is provided, the value is assumed to be Semi-Annual.
Indicates if the cash flows are imported. TRUE indicates that the cash flows are imported. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for imported cash flows.
Indicate Interest only instrument with the value Interest only. This is an input for interest only Commercial real estate and Retail loans.

Describes the cash flow input. The values are Contractual Cash Flow, Principal Cash Flow and Expected Cash Flow. Marked as conditional as this is a required if the cash flow is imported (useImportedCashFlow = TRUE).
Indicates if the Line of Credit is revolving. Values are TRUE for revolving and FALSE for non-revolving. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.

Indicates if the instrument can be cancelled without conditions. TRUE indicates unconditionally cancellable. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit. Default value is set to FALSE.

The instrument type defined by Moody's Analytics according to the type of contractual terms agreed upon between the parties. Valid values are Bond, CDS, Deposit, Equity, Loan, Line of Credit, Receivable, Derivative, and Structured Product. Receivable includes reinsurance receivables. Bond includes all securities except those under Structured Product.
The values supported in ImpairmentStudio are: Bond, Line of Credit, Loan, Receivable, and Structured Product.

The sub type for the instrument type. For Instrument Type Loan or Line of Credit, the valid values are 'Commercial and industrial', Commercial real estate, and Retail. For Instrument Type Receivable, the valid values are Equipment Trust, Lease receivable and Trade receivable. For Instrument Type Bond, valid values are: Corporate, Industrial Revenue, Muni General obligation bonds, Muni Revenue bonds, and Sovereign bond. For Instrument Type Structured Product, valid values are: ABS, CDO, CLO, CMO, CMBS, MBS, Private MBS, and RMBS. Note: Single quote used to enclose the full value. It should not be included in the imported value.

Indicates the segment for the Historical Loss Analyzer (HLA). This is a user defined value to segment the portfolio. Example values are the balance amount item numbers in reporting forms such as 1.a and 1.c.2 or custom names.
The breakdown of the portfolio for reporting purposes. Valid values are: Residential mortgage, Consumer loan, Commercial business, and Commercial mortgage.

Additional breakdown of the portfolio for reporting purposes. Valid values are: 'Commercial and Industrial', Commercial real estate-construction, Commercial real estate-other, Consumer-credit card, Consumer-other, Consumer-auto, Residential-prime, Residential-subprime. Note: Single quote used to enclose the full value. It should not be included in the imported value.

The category describing the consumer product.
For the Expected Consumer Credit Loss Models, the valid values are: Auto Bank Lease, Auto Bank Loan, Auto Finance Lease, Auto Finance Loan, Bankcard, Consumer Finance Installment, Consumer Finance Revolving, First Mortgage, HELOAN, HELOC, Retail Card, Student Loan, and Other.
For Balance Sheet Risk Management, the valid values are: Reverse Mortgage, Solar, and Student Loan.
The type of loan recognized by Moody's models. Valid values are Term Loan, Revolver, and Line.

When marked as conditional, this is required for the disclosure reports. The type of product for which the instrument is reported. For loans, valid values are: Real estate-construction, Real estate-mortgage, Installment loans to individuals, Lease financing, 'Governments and official institutions', 'Banks and other financial institutions', Other loans, and 'Commercial, financial, and agricultural'. For bonds, valid values are: US Treasury, US Agency, Corporate Bonds, Municipal Bonds, and Other Bonds. Note: Single quote used to enclose the full value. It should not be included in the imported value.
Used to describe the allowance for reporting purposes. Valid values are: Domestic, Foreign, and Unallocated. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.

Date the deal was originated or the lease commenced. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
For bonds, marked as conditional as this is a required input for the disclosure reports. For bonds, set the value with the issue date.

The contractual maturity date of the instrument or the expected maturity for those instruments without a contractual maturity date. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the maturity date cannot be derived from Year to Maturity.
The date that the facility or loan commitment is to be renewed. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The last day on which the borrower could draw from the line of credit. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.
The end date of the prepayment penalty period. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the loan has a prepayment penalty.

The original amount of the transaction expressed in instrument currency. For loans, the amount is the amount at origination. For lines of credit, this amount is the commitment amount at origination. Valid values are equal to or greater than 0.
For backward compatibility, it may be the (current) commitment amount of the line of credit. Going forward, the current commitment amount should be imported in current commitment amount.
For lines of credit, this is also required for the MA CI Loss Rate model.
For the Portfolio Analyzer Personal Loans model, this is marked as conditional as this is required for Installment Loans.

The amount the borrower draws from the line of credit when it is originated. Expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are 0 and greater and equal to or less than the Maximum Commitment Amount. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.

Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The annual rate the borrower would take from the Home Equity Line of Credit after the loan is originated.
For Portfolio Studio, the current usage rate. Its valid values are between 0 and 1.
The log of the balance at origination.
The bucket for the log of the balance at origination.

The country of the headquarters for the entity's (borrower's) or its incorporation country where the obligor/assuming entity has a legal representation. For an entity that is a branch, this will be the country of the parent entity. Valid values are the 3 letter country codes from ISO (https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html). This is an input for the PD converter models such as the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter and MA Sovereign TTC to PIT PD Converter.
For entity financial statement, this is a required field.
For Loan Monitoring, in entity financial statement, this field will be set to USA.
The primary country for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity. Valid values are the 3 letter country codes from ISO (https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html).

The location where the debt is issued or the country of risk operations. The valid values are the ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.
When marked as conditional, this or the RiskCalc region, is a required input for the MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, Emerging Markets (EMM 3.1) model.

The region used in the RiskCalc model. Valid values are Africa, Asia, Australia, Canada, MiddleAndEasternEurope, MiddleEast, SouthAmerica, WesternEurope, and US. When marked as conditional, this or the Geography Code, is a required input for the MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, Emerging Markets (EMM 3.1) model. After running the analysis, the MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter may provide the region the model used and it may not be a value listed above.

The amortization type of the instrument. Valid values are Bullet, Linear, Constant Installment, and Other. Bullet is a single principal payment at maturity. Linear is fixed principal only - the same amount of principal paid every period is calculated assuming the loan will be amortized fully at maturity or amortization end date. Constant installment is another word for mortgage - the payment is fixed, but the payment is used to pay back both interest and principal. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. Other is to indicate imported cash flow and thus will not generate cash flows.
Additional valid values are Constant Principal Payment, Increasing Principal Payment, Constant Increasing Principal Payment, and Sum of Digit. These values are valid for the cash flow engine model.

The date of the first contractual principal payment. It is also the contractual date on which the interest only period ends. Before this date, only the interest on the principal balance is paid with the principal balance remaining unchanged. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if amortization does not start at origination or if the instrument has an interest only period.

The contractual date of the last principal payment expected. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. This is a required input if amortization does not end on the maturity date.

The contractual amortization (principal) frequency. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Monthly, Bi-monthly, Bi-weekly, Weekly, Daily and Bullet. The Amortization Frequency (amortizationFrequency) Bullet is valid only for Amortization Type (amortizationType) Bullet.
The original amortization term of the instrument expressed in months.
The term remaining over which the instrument will be amortized, expressed in months.

Indicates if this instrument is negatively amortizing. TRUE means negatively amortizing. For Line of Credit or Fixed rate instruments, this must not be provided or be FALSE. Marked as conditional as this is required to be set to TRUE if the instrument amortizes negatively.

The interest rate type of the instrument. Also known as the coupon type. Valid values are Fixed, Variable, and Other. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for disclosure reports and/or cash flow generation. Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest rate for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest rate is not provided.

Date when the interest rate changes from fixed to variable rate or vice versa. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. A valid valuea for the interest rate type change date is a date between the amortization start date and amortization end date. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the interest rate type changes.

Frequency of the interest payment due. For loans and lines of credits that are not Commercial real estate or retail, this is also the principal frequency and the interest rate reset frequency. Also known as the coupon frequency. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Monthly, Bi-monthly, Bi-weekly, Weekly, Daily and Bullet. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. For non cash flow generation, the maturity date and the interest payment frequency is used to construct the time ruler for cash flow.

The coupon rate for fixed rate instruments. This interest rate remains constant. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation.
Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest rate for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest rate is not provided.
For the Portfolio Analyzer Personal Loans model, this is marked as conditional as this is required for Installment Loans.

The spread over the reference interest rate index/curve that is used for the calculation of the coupon cash flows. Also known as the interest rate variance from the index rate charged. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Required if the interest rate type is Variable. Should not be provided if interest rate type is Fixed. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest spread for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest spread is not provided.
The interest rate for the first payment. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The interest rate for the first payment from reporting date until the next interest rate reset date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
For Relationship Pricing Analyzer, marked as conditional as this is required to compute the deposit expense for deposit accounts.

For variable rate instruments, the interest rate curve/index that will be used to generate coupon cash flows. Required if the interest rate type is Variable. Should not be provided if interest rate type is Fixed. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest spread for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest spread is not provided.
The US interest rate indices that are delivered in the macroeconomic scenarios are listed in the ImpairmentStudio Data Integration Guide, in the section Moody's Analytics Macroeconomic Variables. You can also use the Data Viewer and run a query to search for the macroeconomic variable names that are of macroeconomic variable type Interest Rate.
The multiplier applied to the interest rate index when calculating the coupon. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The credit spread at origination. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The bucket for the credit spread initial.

The spread over the treasury yield curve. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between -1 and 1. For Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer, if this is provided, Fair Value (instrumentReference.fairValueOverride) and Effective Interest Rate(instrumentReference.effectiveInterestRateInitial or instrumentScenario.effectiveInterestRate) must not be provided.

The frequency at which the interest rate is reset after the initial fixed-rate period, if any. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Weekly, Discount, Monthly, Daily, 2-Year, 3-Year, 5-Year, and 10-Year. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Do not provide this for instruments that are not Commercial real estate or Retail. Daily, 2-Year, 3-Year, 5-Year, and 10-Year are not valid values for cash flow generation. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the interest rate resets.

The frequency at which the interest rate is reset after the initial fixed-rate period, if any. Expressed in whole months. For example 1 month is 1. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics and Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer if the interest rate resets.
Initial cap, for ARM loans only. At the initial reset date, the loan rate cannot change by more than this amount. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1
Periodic cap. After the first reset, the loan rate cannot change by more than this amount. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1

The maximum value for the interest rate charged over its contractual term. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for variable rate loans. For portfolio analyzer models, it's lifetime cap. The rate on an ARM loan cannot exceed lifetime CAP + the initial loan rate.

The minimum value for the interest rate charged over its contractual term. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are -0.5 or greater. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for variable rate loans.

The outstanding contractual principal balance on the reporting date. For a Line of Credit, this is the used/drawn amount. For defaulted instruments, this amount is as of the default date. The amount is expressed in instrument currency.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation and disclosure reporting.
For ImpairmentStudio, the unpaid principal balance is expected to be a positive value.

The current commitment amount of the line of credit expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are greater than 0. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.

The unused portion of lines of credit or the unfunded balance on a loan commitment expressed in instrument currency.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for the instrument type Line of Credit if the unpaid principal balance is not provided. It is recommended to provide unpaid principal balance.

The cumulative credit amount drawn from the origination date to the as of date of a non-revolving line of credit expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are 0 or greater.
When marked as conditional, this is an input for the instrument type is Line of Credit.

In instrumentReference, this is the remaining exposure (outstanding balance or for lines of credit, the current commitment amount) expressed in instrument currency. For the MA Expected Consumer Credit Loss models, this is the value for the cohort and expressed in USD.
In instrument risk metric, this amount is the relevant balance value to use for calculating rates for Capital Risk Analyzer.
The reset date for variable rate loans which includes variable loans and loans that change from fixed to variable or vice versa. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The delinquency status of the segment. This is a model input and its valid values are those expected by the model.

Indicates if the property has been foreclosed. Values are TRUE if the property is foreclosed. Marked as a conditional input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) as this is required if the property is foreclosed.

For IFRS, this equals the gross carrying amount. For CECL, this equals the amortized cost. If provided, this amount overrides the calculated value. This amount is expressed in instrument currency. The amortized cost is the amount at which a financing receivable or investment is originated or acquired, adjusted for applicable accrued interest, accretion, or amortization of premiums, discounts, and net deferred fees or costs, collection of cash, write offs, foreign exchange, and fair value hedge adjustments.

The price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at origination or acquisition. If provided, this amount overrides the calculated value.  This is the dirty price. This amount is expressed in instrument currency.

The initial acquisition value adjusted by principal pay down and amortization and accretion of the premium/discount. If provided, this amount overrides the calculated value. This amount is expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for IFRS9.

The annualized through the cycle probability of default 1 year from the reporting of date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Either provide this or Moody's Long Term Rating for PD for the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter. Also used with the TTC Annualized LGD One Year to calculate the incurred loss rate.
The annualized through the cycle probability of default 5 years from the reporting of date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The annualized through the cycle loss given default 1 year from reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Also used with the TTC Annualized PD One Year to calculate the incurred loss rate.

Indicates at what level income, asset, and employment are verified. Valid values are: Full Income - Full Assets, Full Income - Part Assets, Full Income - No Assets, Part Income - Stated Assets, Stated Income - Part Assets, No Income - Partial Assets, No Income - Stated Assets, No Income - No Assets - VOE, and No Income - No Assets - No VOE.

The regulatory rating defined from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC). Valid values are Pass, Watch, Special Mention, Substandard, Doubtful, and Loss.
The valid values for the MA CI Loss Rate model are: Pass, Watch, Special Mention, or Substandard.
The mapping between the regulatory rating and the ratings system are bank specific.
The internal rating at origination.
The internal rating.
The internal rating for the LGD.
The borrower's internal credit score at origination. For the MA Expected Current Credit Losses models, the value is the initial FICO score..

The range in which the borrower's internal score at origination lies. When used with the loss rate models, the expected values are those for Risk Score Band which are 300-529, 530-579, 580-619, 620-659, 660-699, 700-719, 720-739, 740-779, 780-809, 810-850, and MISSING.
The obligor's/assuming entity's internal credit score.

The range in which the obligor's/assuming entity's internal score lies. When used with the loss rate models, the expected values are those for Risk Score Band which are 300-529, 530-579, 580-619, 620-659, 660-699, 700-719, 720-739, 740-779, 780-809, 810-850, and MISSING.
The borrower's FICO credit score at origination.
The borrower's FICO credit score.
The date that the FICO score was issued. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The range in which the borrower's FICO score lies.

Attribute Display Name: Moody's Long Term Rating for PD
Description:
The Moody's long term rating.
For the following use cases, it is the issuer rating.

This rating is used for PD conversion. Either provide this or TTC Annualized PD One Year for the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter.

For the MA Private Firm TTC Rating to TTC PD mapping, this is the input rating.

For Capital Risk Analyzer, provide either this or PD One Year for the EBA analysis.
For structured product instruments, this is the current tranche rating.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for the disclosure reports.

Attribute Display Name: Moody's Long Term Rating for LGD
Description:
The Moody's long term rating to be used for LGD.
For structured products, the value is the original tranche rating and is imported into instrument reference.
The date that the rating was issued. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The Moody's long term rating provided when the security was acquired.
The Moody's long term rating provided at origination or issuance.

The credit quality for investments used for Moody's models. Valid values are: Combined, Investment Grade, Non-Investment Grade. When marked as conditional, this is a required input for the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models.
Indicates to use the refinance grid when calculating the refinance risk for this instrument. Set the value to TRUE if the refinance grid is used.

Indicates whether to use the net operating income (NOI) forecast model to project collateral cash flows. Set to TRUE to use the NOI forecast model. Set to FALSE to use the market average to calculate the collateral cash flow. If no value is provided, FALSE is assumed.

Indicates whether to use the net operating income (NOI) forecast model to project collateral cash flows. Set to TRUE to use the NOI forecast model. Set to FALSE to use the market average to calculate the collateral cash flow. If no value is provided, FALSE is assumed.

The fraction of the instrument that is owned. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are from 0 to 1. Marked as conditional as this is required when the full amounts are provided.
Cash reserves that can be used to make payments expressed in the instrument currency.

The accounting standards classification for impairment. Valid values are: Collectively Evaluated and Individually Evaluated.
This is value is required for import. Prior to the import requirement, if no value was provided, impairment will be considered as Collectively Evaluated.

Indicates if debt restructuring has been applied. TRUE indicates restructuring is applied. A restructuring of a debt constitutes a troubled debt restructuring if the creditor for economic or legal reasons related to the debtorâ€™s financial difficulties grants a concession to the debtor that it would not otherwise consider.
The date the instrument was acquired (or purchased or settled). Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

Indicates if the instrument is classified as a purchased financial asset with credit deterioration (PCD assets). TRUE indicates PCD asset and FALSE indicate non-PCD Assets. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.
The purchase price of the instrument expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports for purchased credit deteriorated (PCD) instruments.

The par value of the instrument when the instrument was acquired expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports for purchased credit deteriorated (PCD) instruments.
The allowance at acquisition expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports for purchase credit deteriorated (PCD) instruments.

The discount/premium amount as of the portfolio as of date expressed in instrument currency. This amount is negative for the discount and positive for the premium. When as marked conditional, this is required for the disclosure reports and defaulted instruments.

The effective interest rate as of the origination/acquisition date. This is the rate that exactly discounts estimated future cash payments or receipts through the expected life of the instrument to its amortized cost. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between -0.9 and 2.
When the value is 0, the expected loss is calculated without the affect of discounting.
Marked as conditional as this is a required input for discounting cash flows when the Loss Discount Rate is Original EIR. If the Loss Discount Rate is Current Prepayment Adjusted EIR, this value is not required.

The effective interest spread as of the origination/acquisition date. This is the spread that exactly discounts estimated future cash payments or receipts through the expected life of the instrument to its amortized cost. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between -1 and 10.
Marked as conditional as this an input for variable rate loans for determining the initial effective interest spread for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest rate is not provided. If the Loss Discount Rate is Current Prepayment Adjusted EIR, this is not required.

The classification and measurement for financial assets under the U.S. GAAP rules. The valid values for loans are: Held for investment (HFI), Held for sale (HFS), and Fair value option (FVO). The valid values for debt securities are: Trading, Held to maturity (HTM), and Available for sale (AFS). Other is a valid value when the instrument does not fall into the criteria of the given values.

The classification of the instrument in accordance with the IFRS accounting standard. Valid values are Financial assets at amortized cost, Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income, Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss, Financial assets held for trading, and Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for IFRS9.

Indicates if the instrument is secured or unsecured. Valid values are Secured and Unsecured.
For the Portfolio Analyzer Personal Loans model, this is marked as conditional as this is required for Installment Loans.

The type of debt seniority recognized by Moody's models. Valid values are Subordinate Bond, Junior Subordinate Bond, Senior Subordinate Bond, Senior Secured Bond, Equipment Trust, Senior Unsecured Bond, Industrial Revenue Bond, Secured Term Loan, Unsecured Term Loan, Secured Revolver, Unsecured Revolver, Mortgage Bond, Municipal Bond, and Preferred Stock.
This is a required input for the RiskCalc LGD, MIS LGD Corporate, and the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models. For the MA CI Proxy PG/LGD models, only the Term Loan and Revolver values are valid.
Specifies the borrower's capital structure at the time of default. Valid values are Has Debt Above, Most Senior Debt, and Unknown.

Indicates the lien position. The order of lien position will be based on this attribute sorted ascending. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics for cross collateralized loans. If provided, this will also be an input for the MIS LGD Corporate model.

For the MA Expected Consumer Credit Loss models, vintages prior to 2005 does not adhere to the ImpairmentStudio calculated vintage. Additional costs such as legal fees and brokerage fees incurred by the lender in selling a foreclosed home expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are 0 or greater.
The loan to value (LTV) at which the insurance policy is terminated expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The likelihood that insurance will not be applied to collateral losses (usually as a result of material contractual errors or similar issues, i.e., rescission). Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The frequency at which premiums are paid to a mortgage insurer. Valid values are Annual, Quarterly, and Monthly.
The premium paid to the mortgage insurer, paid in any one year, as a fraction of the original loan amount. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.
A one-time premium paid to the mortgage insurer.Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.
TRUE indicates if the insurance premiums are refunded pro-rata.
Time from origination over which Upfront Premium may be refunded pro-rata. Expressed in whole months. For example 1 month is 1. Valid values are 1 or greater.

The time it takes for the property to be liquidated after default. This must be provided if the loan has default and a default date is provided. Expressed in whole months. For example 1 month is 1. Valid values are between 0 and 60.
The age of the instrument as of reporting date. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5.
The age of the instrument reflected as a fraction of its total term. Expressed as a decimal. For example 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The bucket for the age ratio.

The number of years till the instrument matures. This also reflects the effective maturity when the maturity date is not provided. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 200 or less. Marked as conditional as this is an input from which the maturity date can be derived. Also, marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports. If maturity date is provided and year to maturity is not provided, year to maturity will be calculated from the maturity date.
The effective life of the instrument expressed in number of months.
The bucket describing the term of the instrument. The values vary by consumer product category. View the list of valid values in the application.
The effective maturity date of the instrument. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. If year to maturity is provided, this value its corresponding date. Otherwise, the value is that from maturity date.
The weighted average life for the instrument. Expressed in number of months.
The remaining term to maturity formatted as string . Examples are Due in <= 1 Year, Due in 1-5 years, Due in 5-10 Years, and Due After 10 Years.
The year from the origination date.

ImpairmentStudio calculates the vintage of the instrument from the origination date. Its format is as yyyyQ#, where # is 1, 2, 3, or 4. For the MA Expected Consumer Credit Loss models, vintages prior to 2005 does not adhere to the ImpairmentStudio calculated vintage. For vintages prior to 2005, you must import these vintage values: 1990Q3 for instruments originated prior to 1990, 1995Q3 for origination between 1990 to 1995, and the year + Q3 for 1996 to 2004 (i.e. 1996Q3, 2004Q3).

The vintage used for reporting. This value is the year of the origination date except for these cases. For revolving instruments, the value is Revolving loans. For instruments where the period between the origination date and the reporting date is greater than 5 years, the value is Prior.
The identifier of the analysis from which the results were calculated or the data was used in.
Unique Name of template. Required field
Term of the instrument, in years, from origination to maturity date

The instrument identifier that links the line of credit and its draws. If the instrument is the Line of Credit, this value is the same as its instrument identifier. This is marked as conditional as this is required for line of credit or loans drawn from a line of credit.

The group to which the loan and property belongs. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) when there is more than one loan and one property in the group.
The date when the financial period ends. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The description of the group to which the loan and property belongs.
The identifier from the loan accounting system.

An identifier to uniquely identify each entity. Each entity must have one entity identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the entity identifier for any other entity. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information.
The Moody's Analytics counterparty identifier.

The obligated party (bond issuer, borrower, debtor, insurer, etc.) who has an obligation to repay a debt, make a payment, do something, or to refrain from doing something, under the terms of an agreement. For reinsurance receivable, this value is the reinsurer or assuming entity's identifier. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information. Marked as conditional as this input is required for reinsurance receivables and the value is the reinsurer or assuming entity's identifier.

An identifier to uniquely identify each instrument. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument. For reinsurance receivable, this value is the treaty identifier. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information.

Type that describes the entity. Valid values include Private Firm, Public Firm, Sovereign, Municipality, Supranational, and SME. Unknown is used when the entity type is not known.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for RiskCalc LGD and to create the scope for determining which Moody's analytics models to use, such as the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, MA Sovereign TTC to PIT PD Converter, and Muni Loss Forecast Model.
The description.

An identifier to identify the portfolio that each instrument belongs. All portfolio data are linked by its portfolio. Example values are CRE, Retail, etc. When marked as conditional, this is an required input if data is provided in the category (such as instrumentCashFlow).
The currency denomination of instruments. Valid values are ISO 4217 currency codes.
The company (branch, holding company, etc.) that this instrument belongs to.

An identifier to identify the portfolio that each instrument belongs. All portfolio data are linked by its portfolio. Example values are CRE, Retail, etc. When marked as conditional, this is an required input if data is provided in the category (such as instrumentCashFlow).
Import source of the data.
The institution who issues the security.

The state or province where the issuer is located. Valid values are 2 letter state abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apb.htm or 2 letter province abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apa_005.htm.
Marked as conditional as this is a required input for the MA Muni Loss Forecast model when the CUSIP is not provided.

Indicate that the instrument is issued and guaranteed by the agency. Valid values are: Farm Credit System Financial Assistance Corporation, Federal Farm Credit Banks, Federal Home Loan Banks, Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation, Federal National Mortgage Association, Government National Mortgage Association, Small Business Administration, and US Government. If no value is provided, the issuing agency is a Private Label. Marked as conditional as this is required for Commercial Mortgage Metrics if the issuing agency is Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation or Federal National Mortgage Association. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.
The issue date of the security. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

Identifier for the scenario. Its value should not include blank spaces.
For inputs, the scenario describes the assumptions used to produce the inputs. If there are no assumptions, this field should be filled with 0. 0 will be used to retrieve model and market data inputs when no scenarios are selected in scenario settings. For outputs, it is the scenario used to produce the results. In addition, Moody's Analytics uses the value Summary for the weighted average results.
The name of the deal.
The date that the deal snapshot was updated.

CUSIP is an acronym that refers to Committee on Uniform Security Identification Procedures. It is an alphanumeric value. The nine-digit CUSIP identifies securities. American Bankers Association CUSIP Database provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence LLC. Copyright 2019. All rights reserved.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input to lookup up model inputs such as the long term rating, PD, R-squared parameters, etc.

The International Securities Identification Number (ISIN) is a code that uniquely identifies a specific securities issue. When marked as conditional, this is a required input to lookup up model inputs such as the long term rating, PD, R-squared parameters, etc.
The original balance of the security expressed in instrument currency.
Indicates if the result is adjusted. TRUE indicates the result is adjusted. FALSE indicates the unadjusted result.
The current balance of the security at reporting date expressed in instrument currency.

A share is an indivisible unit of capital, expressing the ownership relationship between the company and the shareholder. When marked as conditional, this is a required input for the disclosure reports.

Custom attribute for asset class.
Contains the FFIEC Code (index 55) value from the the Interagency Loan Data (ILD) file.

Custom attribute for asset class.
Contains the Type (index 54) value from the the Interagency Loan Data (ILD) file.

Custom attribute for asset class.
Contains the Collateral Code (index 32) value from the the Interagency Loan Data (ILD) file.
The categorization of the adjustment. Values are: Economic/Business Conditions, Historical Loss Experience, Lending Procedures, Nature and Volume of Portfolio, and Other.

Indicates the type of default. Valid values are 180DaysPastDue, 270DaysPastDue, 90DaysPastDue, 90DaysTimesPastDue, AdministratorAppointed, Bailout, Bankruptcy, BankruptcyChapter10, BankruptcyChapter11, BankruptcyChapter7, BankruptcyChapter9, BankruptcyLiquidation, ChargedOff, CollateralPropertyAttached, CompositionProceeding, DaysPastDue, DaysPastDuePassRating, Dissolution, Doubtful, Extinction, FacilitiesCalled, Insolvency, Liquidation, LoanLossProvision, Loss, Moratorium, NonaccrualLoan, OLEM, Receivership, Reorganization, RestructuredDebt, SoftDefault, SpecificLloanLossReserveCreated, Substandard, Substandard30DPD, Substandard60DPD, Unknown, UnlikelyToPay, VoluntaryArrangement, VoluntaryLiquidation, and WindingUp.
Marked as conditional as this is a required input for RiskCalc LGD to determine if the borrower is in bankruptcy.

The state or province where the borrower is located. Valid values are 2 letter state abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apb.htm or 2 letter province abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apa_005.htm.
The zip code where the borrower is located.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) 2017 primary industry for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity.
When marked as conditional, this a required input to lookup the Moody's sector (i.e. GCorr sector) for use in models such as the GCorr, MA CI Proxy PD/LGD and Loss Rate models, MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, and RiskCalc LGD.

The Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) is a four-digit code for the primary industry for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input to lookup the Moody's sector (i.e. GCorr sector).

More detail to describe the reason for the adjustment. Values are: Economic Conditions, External Factors, Future Economic Conditions, Historical Loss Experience, Political Risk, Competition, Lending Management, Underwriting, Loan Review Quality, Concentrations, Problem Loans, Value of Collateral, and Other.

The sectors for municipalities. Valid values are: Airports, Healthcare, Higher Education, Housing, Local Governments, Not-For-Profit, Public Power, Ports, Public Power, States, Toll Facilities, and Water & Sewer. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for the MA Muni Loss Forecast model when the CUSIP is not provided.

The industry or sector recognized by Moody's models. Valid values are Agriculture, ArtsAndEntertainment, Auto, Business_Products, Business_Services, Communication, Construction, Consumer_Products, Finance, Education, Health_Care, HiTech, Manufacturing, Mining, Other, Other_Services, Services, Trade, Transportation, Unassigned, and Utilities.
The values listed support the following models: MA CI Loss Rate, MA CI Proxy PD/LGD, and MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter. For each model, it is a required input and each model uses a subset of the values listed above. The ImpairmentStudio Data Integration Guide provides the sector values that each model supports.
If this field is set to a Moody's industry sector value that is not supported by the specific Moody's model, the model use the value Unasssigned.

The Moody's LGD sector. Valid values are: AerospaceMeasure, BanksAndSL, CableTVPrintingPublishing, ConsumerGoodsAndDurables, Equipment, General, HighTech, MaterialsExtraction, Medical, Municipals, OtherFinancials, REIT, Sovereign, Transportation, Unknown, and Utilities.
This is required for the RiskCalc LGD model.

Total sales of the borrower. The amount entered in instrument or entity reference must be in USD. The amount in the financial statement is in the currency for the financial statement. When marked as conditional, the value in instrument reference is a required input to lookup the GCorr R-squared parameters for private non financial firms.

The total assets of the borrower which is the sum of the current assets, plus net property, plant, and equipment, plus other non-current assets (including intangible assets, deferred items, and investments and advances). The amount entered in instrument or entity reference must be in USD. The amount in the financial statement is in the currency for the financial statement. The amount in instrument reference is a required input for the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models as it is used to calculate the Total Assets Size Bucket. When marked as conditional, the amount in instrument reference is a required input to lookup the GCorr R-squared parameters for private financial firms.

Indicates the intent to sell. TRUE indicates the intention to sell as well as the likelihood that the security will be required to be sold before recovery. For the current expected loss calculation (CECL), this is required to be set to TRUE if there is an intent to sell. Default is FALSE.
Indicate accrual or non-accrual assets. Valid values are: Accrual assets and Non-accrual assets. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.

The compounding frequency used to calculate the fair value. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Discount and Monthly.
For interest rate input, this is marked as conditional as this is a required input if the interest rate curve form is SPOT. If no value is provided, the value is assumed to be Semi-Annual.
Indicates if the cash flows are imported. TRUE indicates that the cash flows are imported. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for imported cash flows.
Indicate Interest only instrument with the value Interest only. This is an input for interest only Commercial real estate and Retail loans.
The scope for which the loss estimation method rule is applied.

Describes the cash flow input. The values are Contractual Cash Flow, Principal Cash Flow and Expected Cash Flow. Marked as conditional as this is a required if the cash flow is imported (useImportedCashFlow = TRUE).
Indicates if the Line of Credit is revolving. Values are TRUE for revolving and FALSE for non-revolving. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.

Indicates if the instrument can be cancelled without conditions. TRUE indicates unconditionally cancellable. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit. Default value is set to FALSE.

The instrument type defined by Moody's Analytics according to the type of contractual terms agreed upon between the parties. Valid values are Bond, CDS, Deposit, Equity, Loan, Line of Credit, Receivable, Derivative, and Structured Product. Receivable includes reinsurance receivables. Bond includes all securities except those under Structured Product.
The values supported in ImpairmentStudio are: Bond, Line of Credit, Loan, Receivable, and Structured Product.

The sub type for the instrument type. For Instrument Type Loan or Line of Credit, the valid values are 'Commercial and industrial', Commercial real estate, and Retail. For Instrument Type Receivable, the valid values are Equipment Trust, Lease receivable and Trade receivable. For Instrument Type Bond, valid values are: Corporate, Industrial Revenue, Muni General obligation bonds, Muni Revenue bonds, and Sovereign bond. For Instrument Type Structured Product, valid values are: ABS, CDO, CLO, CMO, CMBS, MBS, Private MBS, and RMBS. Note: Single quote used to enclose the full value. It should not be included in the imported value.
The order for the loss estimation method scope.

Indicates the segment for the Historical Loss Analyzer (HLA). This is a user defined value to segment the portfolio. Example values are the balance amount item numbers in reporting forms such as 1.a and 1.c.2 or custom names.
The breakdown of the portfolio for reporting purposes. Valid values are: Residential mortgage, Consumer loan, Commercial business, and Commercial mortgage.

Additional breakdown of the portfolio for reporting purposes. Valid values are: 'Commercial and Industrial', Commercial real estate-construction, Commercial real estate-other, Consumer-credit card, Consumer-other, Consumer-auto, Residential-prime, Residential-subprime. Note: Single quote used to enclose the full value. It should not be included in the imported value.

The category describing the consumer product.
For the Expected Consumer Credit Loss Models, the valid values are: Auto Bank Lease, Auto Bank Loan, Auto Finance Lease, Auto Finance Loan, Bankcard, Consumer Finance Installment, Consumer Finance Revolving, First Mortgage, HELOAN, HELOC, Retail Card, Student Loan, and Other.
For Balance Sheet Risk Management, the valid values are: Reverse Mortgage, Solar, and Student Loan.

When marked as conditional, this is required for the disclosure reports. The type of product for which the instrument is reported. For loans, valid values are: Real estate-construction, Real estate-mortgage, Installment loans to individuals, Lease financing, 'Governments and official institutions', 'Banks and other financial institutions', Other loans, and 'Commercial, financial, and agricultural'. For bonds, valid values are: US Treasury, US Agency, Corporate Bonds, Municipal Bonds, and Other Bonds. Note: Single quote used to enclose the full value. It should not be included in the imported value.

The calculation method used to calculate the expected credit loss.
The methods vary by accounting classification.
For the Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL), the loss estimation methods vary by ASC impairment evaulation. For instruments that are collectively evaluated the valid values are: DCF: Expected Cash Flow, DCF: Loss Cash Flow, Loss Rate, PD/LGD, and Imported Allowance. For instruments that are individually evaluated the valid values are: Collateral Value, DCF: Expected Cash Flow, DCF: Loss Cash Flow, Loan's Fair Value, Loss Rate, PD/LGD, and Imported Allowance.
For the International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), the the valid values are: DCF: Expected Cash Flow, DCF: Loss Cash Flow, PD/LGD, and Imported Allowance.
Used to describe the allowance for reporting purposes. Valid values are: Domestic, Foreign, and Unallocated. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.
The date that the facility or loan commitment is to be renewed. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The last day on which the borrower could draw from the line of credit. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.
The end date of the prepayment penalty period. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the loan has a prepayment penalty.

The amount the borrower draws from the line of credit when it is originated. Expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are 0 and greater and equal to or less than the Maximum Commitment Amount. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.

Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The annual rate the borrower would take from the Home Equity Line of Credit after the loan is originated.
For Portfolio Studio, the current usage rate. Its valid values are between 0 and 1.

The country of the headquarters for the entity's (borrower's) or its incorporation country where the obligor/assuming entity has a legal representation. For an entity that is a branch, this will be the country of the parent entity. Valid values are the 3 letter country codes from ISO (https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html). This is an input for the PD converter models such as the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter and MA Sovereign TTC to PIT PD Converter.
For entity financial statement, this is a required field.
For Loan Monitoring, in entity financial statement, this field will be set to USA.
The primary country for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity. Valid values are the 3 letter country codes from ISO (https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html).

The location where the debt is issued or the country of risk operations. The valid values are the ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.
When marked as conditional, this or the RiskCalc region, is a required input for the MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, Emerging Markets (EMM 3.1) model.

The region used in the RiskCalc model. Valid values are Africa, Asia, Australia, Canada, MiddleAndEasternEurope, MiddleEast, SouthAmerica, WesternEurope, and US. When marked as conditional, this or the Geography Code, is a required input for the MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, Emerging Markets (EMM 3.1) model. After running the analysis, the MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter may provide the region the model used and it may not be a value listed above.

Indicates which effective interest rate is used. Valid values are Original EIR, Current Prepayment Adjusted EIR, or None. Original EIR is an option for both DCF methods. Current Prepayment Adjusted EIR is an option only for DCF: Expected Cash Flow. None is an option only for DCF: Loss Cash Flow.

The amortization type of the instrument. Valid values are Bullet, Linear, Constant Installment, and Other. Bullet is a single principal payment at maturity. Linear is fixed principal only - the same amount of principal paid every period is calculated assuming the loan will be amortized fully at maturity or amortization end date. Constant installment is another word for mortgage - the payment is fixed, but the payment is used to pay back both interest and principal. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. Other is to indicate imported cash flow and thus will not generate cash flows.
Additional valid values are Constant Principal Payment, Increasing Principal Payment, Constant Increasing Principal Payment, and Sum of Digit. These values are valid for the cash flow engine model.

The contractual amortization (principal) frequency. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Monthly, Bi-monthly, Bi-weekly, Weekly, Daily and Bullet. The Amortization Frequency (amortizationFrequency) Bullet is valid only for Amortization Type (amortizationType) Bullet.
The ImpairmentStudio calculation version that produced the allowance result.

Indicates if this instrument is negatively amortizing. TRUE means negatively amortizing. For Line of Credit or Fixed rate instruments, this must not be provided or be FALSE. Marked as conditional as this is required to be set to TRUE if the instrument amortizes negatively.

The interest rate type of the instrument. Also known as the coupon type. Valid values are Fixed, Variable, and Other. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for disclosure reports and/or cash flow generation. Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest rate for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest rate is not provided.

Date when the interest rate changes from fixed to variable rate or vice versa. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. A valid valuea for the interest rate type change date is a date between the amortization start date and amortization end date. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the interest rate type changes.

Attribute for classifying the risk. Valid values are: Credit loss and non-credit loss are recognized, Allowance is capped by the difference of Amortized Cost and Fair Value, Zero credit loss recognized, Credit loss is on amortized cost basis, and Default Asset., Physical, Transition, Combined

Frequency of the interest payment due. For loans and lines of credits that are not Commercial real estate or retail, this is also the principal frequency and the interest rate reset frequency. Also known as the coupon frequency. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Monthly, Bi-monthly, Bi-weekly, Weekly, Daily and Bullet. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. For non cash flow generation, the maturity date and the interest payment frequency is used to construct the time ruler for cash flow.

The coupon rate for fixed rate instruments. This interest rate remains constant. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation.
Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest rate for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest rate is not provided.
For the Portfolio Analyzer Personal Loans model, this is marked as conditional as this is required for Installment Loans.

The spread over the reference interest rate index/curve that is used for the calculation of the coupon cash flows. Also known as the interest rate variance from the index rate charged. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Required if the interest rate type is Variable. Should not be provided if interest rate type is Fixed. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest spread for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest spread is not provided.
The interest rate for the first payment. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The interest rate for the first payment from reporting date until the next interest rate reset date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
For Relationship Pricing Analyzer, marked as conditional as this is required to compute the deposit expense for deposit accounts.

The change in the allowance amount as a result of applying quantitative or qualitative adjustments or applying the calculation. This is the sum of the on and off balance sheet reserves. This amount is reported in the disclosure reports.

For variable rate instruments, the interest rate curve/index that will be used to generate coupon cash flows. Required if the interest rate type is Variable. Should not be provided if interest rate type is Fixed. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation. Also marked as conditional as this an input for determining the initial effective interest spread for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest spread is not provided.
The US interest rate indices that are delivered in the macroeconomic scenarios are listed in the ImpairmentStudio Data Integration Guide, in the section Moody's Analytics Macroeconomic Variables. You can also use the Data Viewer and run a query to search for the macroeconomic variable names that are of macroeconomic variable type Interest Rate.
The change in the allowance as a result of applying quantitative or qualitative adjustments or applying the calculation. Used for IFRS9.

The spread over the treasury yield curve. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between -1 and 1. For Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer, if this is provided, Fair Value (instrumentReference.fairValueOverride) and Effective Interest Rate(instrumentReference.effectiveInterestRateInitial or instrumentScenario.effectiveInterestRate) must not be provided.
The impairment type of the instrument calculated from stage allocation for IFRS9. Valid values are: Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3.
The IFRS Impairment Type calculated from the stage rating or uncondtional PD for stage allocation for IFRS9. Valid values are Stage 1, Stage, 2, and Stage 3.
The IFRS Impairment Type calculated from the scenario based PD for stage allocation for IFRS9. Valid values are Stage 1, Stage, 2, and Stage 3.
The change in the allowance rate as a result of applying quantitative or qualitative adjustments or applying the calculation. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The frequency at which the interest rate is reset after the initial fixed-rate period, if any. Valid values are Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Weekly, Discount, Monthly, Daily, 2-Year, 3-Year, 5-Year, and 10-Year. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Do not provide this for instruments that are not Commercial real estate or Retail. Daily, 2-Year, 3-Year, 5-Year, and 10-Year are not valid values for cash flow generation. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the interest rate resets.

The frequency at which the interest rate is reset after the initial fixed-rate period, if any. Expressed in whole months. For example 1 month is 1. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics and Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer if the interest rate resets.
Initial cap, for ARM loans only. At the initial reset date, the loan rate cannot change by more than this amount. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1
The fraction of the total loss that applies to this instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
Periodic cap. After the first reset, the loan rate cannot change by more than this amount. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1

The maximum value for the interest rate charged over its contractual term. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for variable rate loans. For portfolio analyzer models, it's lifetime cap. The rate on an ARM loan cannot exceed lifetime CAP + the initial loan rate.

The minimum value for the interest rate charged over its contractual term. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are -0.5 or greater. This is an input for the cash flow generation of Commercial real estate and Retail loans. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for variable rate loans.

The outstanding contractual principal balance on the reporting date. For a Line of Credit, this is the used/drawn amount. For defaulted instruments, this amount is as of the default date. The amount is expressed in instrument currency.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for cash flow generation and disclosure reporting.
For ImpairmentStudio, the unpaid principal balance is expected to be a positive value.

The current commitment amount of the line of credit expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are greater than 0. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.
The fraction of the total loss that applies to this instrument by its instrument type. The instruments are categorized as Bonds and non-Bonds. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The unused portion of lines of credit or the unfunded balance on a loan commitment expressed in instrument currency.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for the instrument type Line of Credit if the unpaid principal balance is not provided. It is recommended to provide unpaid principal balance.

The cumulative credit amount drawn from the origination date to the as of date of a non-revolving line of credit expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are 0 or greater.
When marked as conditional, this is an input for the instrument type is Line of Credit.
The reset date for variable rate loans which includes variable loans and loans that change from fixed to variable or vice versa. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

Indicates if the property has been foreclosed. Values are TRUE if the property is foreclosed. Marked as a conditional input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) as this is required if the property is foreclosed.

The price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at origination or acquisition. If provided, this amount overrides the calculated value.  This is the dirty price. This amount is expressed in instrument currency.
The expected credit losses for the on balance sheet credit exposures.

The initial acquisition value adjusted by principal pay down and amortization and accretion of the premium/discount. If provided, this amount overrides the calculated value. This amount is expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for IFRS9.

Attribute Display Name: On Balance Sheet Loss Rate Lifetime
Description: The cumulative lifetime loss rate for the on balance sheet reserve. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The annualized through the cycle probability of default 1 year from the reporting of date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Either provide this or Moody's Long Term Rating for PD for the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter. Also used with the TTC Annualized LGD One Year to calculate the incurred loss rate.
The annualized through the cycle probability of default 5 years from the reporting of date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The expected credit losses for the off balance sheet credit exposures.

The annualized through the cycle loss given default 1 year from reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Also used with the TTC Annualized PD One Year to calculate the incurred loss rate.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet Loss Rate Lifetime
Description: The cumulative lifetime loss rate for the off balance sheet reserve. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

Indicates at what level income, asset, and employment are verified. Valid values are: Full Income - Full Assets, Full Income - Part Assets, Full Income - No Assets, Part Income - Stated Assets, Stated Income - Part Assets, No Income - Partial Assets, No Income - Stated Assets, No Income - No Assets - VOE, and No Income - No Assets - No VOE.

The regulatory rating defined from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC). Valid values are Pass, Watch, Special Mention, Substandard, Doubtful, and Loss.
The valid values for the MA CI Loss Rate model are: Pass, Watch, Special Mention, or Substandard.
The mapping between the regulatory rating and the ratings system are bank specific.
The internal rating at origination.
The internal rating.
The internal rating for the LGD.
The borrower's internal credit score at origination. For the MA Expected Current Credit Losses models, the value is the initial FICO score..
The allowance after applying adjustments.

The range in which the borrower's internal score at origination lies. When used with the loss rate models, the expected values are those for Risk Score Band which are 300-529, 530-579, 580-619, 620-659, 660-699, 700-719, 720-739, 740-779, 780-809, 810-850, and MISSING.
The obligor's/assuming entity's internal credit score.

The range in which the obligor's/assuming entity's internal score lies. When used with the loss rate models, the expected values are those for Risk Score Band which are 300-529, 530-579, 580-619, 620-659, 660-699, 700-719, 720-739, 740-779, 780-809, 810-850, and MISSING.
The borrower's FICO credit score at origination.
The borrower's FICO credit score.
The date that the FICO score was issued. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The range in which the borrower's FICO score lies.

Attribute Display Name: Moody's Long Term Rating for PD
Description:
The Moody's long term rating.
For the following use cases, it is the issuer rating.

This rating is used for PD conversion. Either provide this or TTC Annualized PD One Year for the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter.

For the MA Private Firm TTC Rating to TTC PD mapping, this is the input rating.

For Capital Risk Analyzer, provide either this or PD One Year for the EBA analysis.
For structured product instruments, this is the current tranche rating.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for the disclosure reports.

Attribute Display Name: Moody's Long Term Rating for LGD
Description:
The Moody's long term rating to be used for LGD.
For structured products, the value is the original tranche rating and is imported into instrument reference.
The date that the rating was issued. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The Moody's long term rating provided when the security was acquired.
The Moody's long term rating provided at origination or issuance.

The credit quality for investments used for Moody's models. Valid values are: Combined, Investment Grade, Non-Investment Grade. When marked as conditional, this is a required input for the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models.
Indicates to use the refinance grid when calculating the refinance risk for this instrument. Set the value to TRUE if the refinance grid is used.

Indicates whether to use the net operating income (NOI) forecast model to project collateral cash flows. Set to TRUE to use the NOI forecast model. Set to FALSE to use the market average to calculate the collateral cash flow. If no value is provided, FALSE is assumed.
The allowance before applying adjustments.
