The fraction of the instrument that is owned. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are from 0 to 1. Marked as conditional as this is required when the full amounts are provided.
Cash reserves that can be used to make payments expressed in the instrument currency.

The accounting standards classification for impairment. Valid values are: Collectively Evaluated and Individually Evaluated.
This is value is required for import. Prior to the import requirement, if no value was provided, impairment will be considered as Collectively Evaluated.

Indicates if debt restructuring has been applied. TRUE indicates restructuring is applied. A restructuring of a debt constitutes a troubled debt restructuring if the creditor for economic or legal reasons related to the debtorâ€™s financial difficulties grants a concession to the debtor that it would not otherwise consider.
The date the instrument was acquired (or purchased or settled). Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

Indicates if the instrument is classified as a purchased financial asset with credit deterioration (PCD assets). TRUE indicates PCD asset and FALSE indicate non-PCD Assets. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.
The purchase price of the instrument expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports for purchased credit deteriorated (PCD) instruments.

The par value of the instrument when the instrument was acquired expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports for purchased credit deteriorated (PCD) instruments.
The allowance at acquisition expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports for purchase credit deteriorated (PCD) instruments.

The discount/premium amount as of the portfolio as of date expressed in instrument currency. This amount is negative for the discount and positive for the premium. When as marked conditional, this is required for the disclosure reports and defaulted instruments.

The effective interest rate as of the origination/acquisition date. This is the rate that exactly discounts estimated future cash payments or receipts through the expected life of the instrument to its amortized cost. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between -0.9 and 2.
When the value is 0, the expected loss is calculated without the affect of discounting.
Marked as conditional as this is a required input for discounting cash flows when the Loss Discount Rate is Original EIR. If the Loss Discount Rate is Current Prepayment Adjusted EIR, this value is not required.

The effective interest spread as of the origination/acquisition date. This is the spread that exactly discounts estimated future cash payments or receipts through the expected life of the instrument to its amortized cost. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between -1 and 10.
Marked as conditional as this an input for variable rate loans for determining the initial effective interest spread for DCF: Loss Cash Flow when the initial effective interest rate is not provided. If the Loss Discount Rate is Current Prepayment Adjusted EIR, this is not required.

The classification and measurement for financial assets under the U.S. GAAP rules. The valid values for loans are: Held for investment (HFI), Held for sale (HFS), and Fair value option (FVO). The valid values for debt securities are: Trading, Held to maturity (HTM), and Available for sale (AFS). Other is a valid value when the instrument does not fall into the criteria of the given values.

The classification of the instrument in accordance with the IFRS accounting standard. Valid values are Financial assets at amortized cost, Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income, Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss, Financial assets held for trading, and Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for IFRS9.

Indicates if the instrument is secured or unsecured. Valid values are Secured and Unsecured.
For the Portfolio Analyzer Personal Loans model, this is marked as conditional as this is required for Installment Loans.

The type of debt seniority recognized by Moody's models. Valid values are Subordinate Bond, Junior Subordinate Bond, Senior Subordinate Bond, Senior Secured Bond, Equipment Trust, Senior Unsecured Bond, Industrial Revenue Bond, Secured Term Loan, Unsecured Term Loan, Secured Revolver, Unsecured Revolver, Mortgage Bond, Municipal Bond, and Preferred Stock.
This is a required input for the RiskCalc LGD, MIS LGD Corporate, and the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models. For the MA CI Proxy PG/LGD models, only the Term Loan and Revolver values are valid.
Specifies the borrower's capital structure at the time of default. Valid values are Has Debt Above, Most Senior Debt, and Unknown.
The allowance rate after applying adjustments. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

Indicates the lien position. The order of lien position will be based on this attribute sorted ascending. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics for cross collateralized loans. If provided, this will also be an input for the MIS LGD Corporate model.

Indicates if the terms of the instrument has been modified. TRUE indicates the terms are modified. Marked as conditional as this is required to be TRUE for modified instruments for the disclosure reports and Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer (MPA).

The instrument identifier of the instrument that was modified into this instrument. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.
The outstanding balance of the instrument prior to modification expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.
An indicator that specifies whether a bailout is expected. TRUE indicates a baliout is expected. No value is treated as Unknown.
The allowance rate before applying adjustments. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

When this is marked as conditional, it is required for the disclosure reports. The accrual status based on the delinquency state. Valid values are Non-accrual Assets, 'Greater Than 90 DPD and accruing', and Non-accrual without ACL. Note: Single quote used to enclose the full value. It should not be included in the imported value.

Indicates the status of the instrument. Valid values are New Origination, New Acquisition, Derecognized, Matured, Prepaid, Defaulted, and Modified. When as marked conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports and for Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer, this is required to be set to Prepaid if the instrument has been prepaid. If the value is imported, it is the value used. If the value is not imported, it will be determined by ImpairmentStudio.
Amount charged off net of recovery amount during reporting period expressed in instrument currency.

When marked as conditional, this is required for a defaulted instrument. For the defaulted instrument, this is the amount that has already been charged off. This amount is expressed in instrument currency. This is expected to be provided as a negative value.
The fraction of the liquidation amount lost as expense. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.

Indicates whether there is any guarantee associated with the loan/property group. If any loan within the group is guaranteed, the group is treated as guaranteed. TRUE indicates it is guaranteed. Marked as conditional as this is required to be TRUE if the loan/property group is guaranteed.
The value of the collateral associated with the instrument at the time of origination. Expressed in instrument currency.

The value of the collateral associated with the instrument expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) as this is required when providing property information in property reference. Also, marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.

The incurred loss amount. The loss reserve that is required after a loss event has occurred or its occurrence is probable. For the DCF: Loss Cash Flow method, when projected cash flows are imported or generated outside of ImpairmentStudio, this is one of the imported cash flow amounts. The other is the Loss Amount.
The estimated cost to sell the collateral expressed in instrument currency.

The date in which the collateral value is effective. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Marked as conditional for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) as this is required when providing property information in property reference.
The cash value for additional collateral that is not an income producing commercial real estate associated to the specific instrument. This amount is expressed in the instrument currency.
The city where the collateral is located.

The state or province where the collateral is located. Valid values are 2 letter state abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apb.htm or 2 letter province abbreviations defined here: https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28apa_005.htm. For the loss model, if this is not provided, the national average is used. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for commercial real estate loans or mortgages when collateral zip code is not provided. For Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM), is required when providing property information in property reference.
The incurred loss rate. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The zip code where the collateral is located. For the loss model, if this is not provided, the national average is used. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for commercial real estate loans or mortgages when collateral state is not provided. For Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM), is required when providing property information in property reference.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet Incurred Loss Amount
Description: The incurred loss amount for the off balance sheet item. The loss reserve that is required after a loss event has occurred or its occurrence is probable.

The country in where the collateral is located. Valid values are the 3 letter country codes from ISO (https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html).In additional for Commercial Mortgage Metrics Europe model, an extra value PEU, which indicates it's a Pan European instrument is also available.

The incurred loss rate for the off balance sheet item. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
ImpairmentStudio does not produce a value for this field found in instrument result.
The Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) where the collateral is located.
The description for the property.

Status refers to whether a property is existing and stabilized or still under construction. Valid values are: Existing and Under Construction. Marked as conditional for the MA Loss Rate Model as this is required when providing property information in property reference.

Attribute Display Name: Present Value of Expected Cash Flow
Description: The present value of the expected cash flow.

The expected completion date for the property under construction. Used with the Property Status is Under Construction. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Marked as conditional for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) as this is required when the property status is Under Construction.

The type of business the property is used for. Valid values are Hotel, Industrial, Multifamily, Office, Retail, and Other. For Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM), this is required when providing property information in property reference. The value Other is valid for the MA CRE Loss Rate Model and is not valid for CMM.

Property type reporting field used in disclosures. Valid values are Office Space, Shopping Mall, Residential, Commercial, 1-4 Family, and HELOC. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for disclosure reports.

A subset of the property type for a better description of the business. Valid values for Hotel are Full Service and Limited Service. Valid values for Office are Downtown and Suburban. Valid values for Industrial are All, Light Industrial, Logistics. Valid values for Retail are All, High Street, Shopping Centre.

The residence type for the property. Valid values are Single Family, De minimus planned unit development, Townhouse, Planned unit development, Condominium, Co-op, Two-four family, New construction, Leasehold, Condotel, Manufactured housing, and Other.
The size of the property. For Hotel, its number of rooms. For Mutlifamily, its number of units. For all other property types, its square footage. Valid values are 0 or greater.

Indicates the owner occupancy status. Valid values are: Owner, Non-owner, Second home, Investor, and Rental.
For Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer, Non-owner is not a valid value.

The total lifetime exposure at default (EAD) amount for the instrument. For instruments with no off-balance sheet component, which includes bonds, loans, etc., the total ead amount is equal to the amortized cost override when provided.
The volatility component related to market-wide factors, where the market is defined as property type and metropolitan statistical area. This is applied as a multiplier.

Attribute Display Name: On Balance Sheet EAD Amount Lifetime
Description: The lifetime exposure at default (EAD) amount for the on balance sheet portion of the instrument.
The volatility component related to property-specific and micro-location-specific factors. This is applied as a multiplier.
The volatility component related to one-time operating income shocks not expected to repeat in subsequent periods. This is applied as a multiplier.
For IFRS, this equals the net carrying amount, which is the gross carrying amount less the allowance. For CECL, this equals the amortized cost. This amount is calculated at reporting date.
The net operating income expressed in instrument currency.
The date in which the net operating income is effective. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. This is a required input when net operating income is provided.

The current loan to value. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) when the collateral value and net operating income are not provided. Also, marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports.
The date in which the loan the value is effective. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) when the loan to value is provided.
The loan to value at the time the loan was originated. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The fraction of the total amortized that applies to this instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
This is calculated by net carrying amount.
The bucket in which the loan to value at the time the loan was originated falls in. This is a model input and Its valid values are those expected by the model.
The loan to value of the junior lien on the property at the time the loan was originated. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The debt service coverage ratio. DSCR is useful for measuring the payback ability of the loan. Expressed in decimal format as a ratio. For example, if net operating income is 1.1x the total debt service, this value is 1.1. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for Commercial Mortgage Metrics (CMM) when the collateral value and net operating income are not provided.
The date in which the debt service coverage ratio was provided. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Marked as conditional as this is a required input when the debt service coverage ratio is provided.
The expected constant payment beyond the maturity period of the instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.

Attribute Display Name: Amortized Cost Portion By Instrument Type
Description:
The fraction of the total amortized that applies to this instrument by its instrument type. The instruments are categorized as Bonds and non-Bonds. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. For example, 10% is 0.1.
This is calculated by net carrying amount.
Captures any existing lease agreements for a property under construction. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
Indicates if the loan has mortgage insurance. TRUE indicates the loan has mortgage insurance.

The fraction of the gross loss covered by the insurer. Gross loss includes the unpaid principal balance, lost interest, and additional costs. The amount paid by the insurer is typically lower than the covered percentage of the gross loss due to money received by selling the foreclosed property. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
If loan has primary insurance coverage, this indicates whether the policy payee is the borrower or the lender. TRUE inidicates the policy payee is the borrower.
Additional costs such as legal fees and brokerage fees incurred by the lender in selling a foreclosed home expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are 0 or greater.

The beginning outstanding principal balance for the provided cash flow or reporting.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for importing contractual cash flows and must be expressed in instrument currency.
For results, the value is expressed in reporting currency.
The loan to value (LTV) at which the insurance policy is terminated expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The likelihood that insurance will not be applied to collateral losses (usually as a result of material contractual errors or similar issues, i.e., rescission). Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The frequency at which premiums are paid to a mortgage insurer. Valid values are Annual, Quarterly, and Monthly.
The premium paid to the mortgage insurer, paid in any one year, as a fraction of the original loan amount. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.
A one-time premium paid to the mortgage insurer.Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.
The fraction of the total unpaid principal balance that applies to this instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
TRUE indicates if the insurance premiums are refunded pro-rata.
Time from origination over which Upfront Premium may be refunded pro-rata. Expressed in whole months. For example 1 month is 1. Valid values are 1 or greater.

The time it takes for the property to be liquidated after default. This must be provided if the loan has default and a default date is provided. Expressed in whole months. For example 1 month is 1. Valid values are between 0 and 60.

The number of years till the instrument matures. This also reflects the effective maturity when the maturity date is not provided. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 200 or less. Marked as conditional as this is an input from which the maturity date can be derived. Also, marked as conditional as this is required for the disclosure reports. If maturity date is provided and year to maturity is not provided, year to maturity will be calculated from the maturity date.
The effective life of the instrument expressed in number of months.

The fraction of the total unpaid principal balance that applies to this instrument by its instrument type. The instruments are categorized as Bonds and non-Bonds. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The bucket describing the term of the instrument. The values vary by consumer product category. View the list of valid values in the application.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet EAD Amount Lifetime
Description: The lifetime exposure at default (EAD) amount for the off balance sheet portion of the  instrument.
The weighted average life for the instrument. Expressed in number of months.

ImpairmentStudio calculates the vintage of the instrument from the origination date. Its format is as yyyyQ#, where # is 1, 2, 3, or 4. For the MA Expected Consumer Credit Loss models, vintages prior to 2005 does not adhere to the ImpairmentStudio calculated vintage. For vintages prior to 2005, you must import these vintage values: 1990Q3 for instruments originated prior to 1990, 1995Q3 for origination between 1990 to 1995, and the year + Q3 for 1996 to 2004 (i.e. 1996Q3, 2004Q3).
The annualized probability of default derived from qualitative scorecards. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The allowance amount for the maturity risk.

The model name used to generate the point-in-time (PIT) probability of default (PD) curve. Used with private firm entities only. Valid values are: USA 4.0, UDS 4.0, UNP 4.0, URO 4.0, USF 4.0, LNA 3.1, AUS 4.0, AUT 3.2, BEL 4.0, BNK 4.0, CAN 3.2, CHE 3.1, CHN 3.1, DEU 3.2, DNK 4.0, EMM 3.1, ESP 4.0, FIN 4.0, FRA 4.0, GBR 4.0, GRC 4.0, ITA 4.0, JPN 3.2, LFE 4.0, NLD 4.0, NOR 4.0, PRT 4.0, RUS 3.1, SGP 4.0, SWE 4.0, ZAF 3.2, ILH 3.1, and IPC 3.1.
When marked as conditional, this a required input for the MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter. Check the ImpairmentStudio Data Integration Guide to see the Private Firm Models are supported by each model.

The expected credit loss amount for the life of the instrument from the quantitative calculation.
For results, expressed in reporting currency.
For non-results, expressed in instrument currency.

The unconditional point in time probability of default (PD) 1 year from reporting date. The credit cycle adjusted PD. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
Marked as conditional as value is required when an unconditional PD is required but the PD model selected produces conditional PDs.
When the PD source is Instrument PD, this is the corresponding PD value. If provided along with the LGD one year, these are used to calculate the incurred loss rate for the loss estimation method is PD/LGD. Otherwise, the incurred loss rate is calculated from the TTC Annualized PD One Year and TTC Annualized LGD One Year.
For Capital Risk Analyzer, provide either this or Moody's Long Term Rating for PD for the EBA analysis.

A value of TRUE indicates that the PD maturity risk is excluded when mean reversion is applied. If the value is FALSE or no value is imported, the PD maturity risk is included when mean reversion is applied.

The annualized probability of default derived from quantitative models or mapped from the rating produced in the scorecard. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

Attribute Display Name: Expected Credit Loss Amount at Maturity
Description: The expected credit loss amount for the maturity risk for the instrument. This amount is included in the instrument's expected credit loss amount.

The annualized probability of default implied from the master rating scale . Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
Moodyâ€™s Analytics Market Implied Ratings convert the market assessment of a given company's credit risk into rating terms, enabling you to easily compare credit signals and agency ratings. However, the Moodyâ€™s Analytics Market Implied Ratings do not represent or constitute actual Moodyâ€™s Investors Service, Inc. (MIS) ratings, rating actions or opinions.
Constant added to the probability of default output. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.
The expected credit loss for the life of the instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The loss given default value 1 year from the reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. When the LGD source is Instrument LGD, LGD One Year in Instrument Reference is the LGD value. If provided along with the PD one year, these are used to calculate the incurred loss rate for the loss estimation method is PD/LGD. Otherwise, the incurred loss rate is calculated from the TTC Annualized PD One Year and TTC Annualized LGD One Year. The other use of this value is for when an unconditional LGD is required but the LGD model selected produces conditional LGDs. For the result, this value is the LGD used to calculate the one year expected credit loss amount.

A value of TRUE indicates that the LGD maturity risk is excluded when mean reversion is applied. If the value is FALSE or no value is imported, the LGD maturity risk is included when mean reversion is applied.
The annualized loss given default derived from quantitative models. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The annualized loss given derived from qualitative scorecards. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The annualized loss given default implied from the master rating scale . Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
Moodyâ€™s Analytics Market Implied Ratings convert the market assessment of a given company's credit risk into rating terms, enabling you to easily compare credit signals and agency ratings. However, the Moodyâ€™s Analytics Market Implied Ratings do not represent or constitute actual Moodyâ€™s Investors Service, Inc. (MIS) ratings, rating actions or opinions.

Attribute Display Name: Expected Credit Loss Amount One Year
Description:
The expected credit loss amount 1 year from the reporting date.
For results, expressed in reporting currency.
For non-results, expressed in instrument currency.
The LGD floor overrides the calculated LGD when it is less than this value. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater.

The average time for loss confirmation (time difference between the first loss event and the charge-off date) under the incurred loss model. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0 or greater.

The loss rate which is calculated on an amortized cost basis based on the term to maturity. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
For ImpairmentStudio, this is marked as conditional as this is a required input for the instrument lifetime loss rate and for the scenario based lifetime loss rate.
For Relationship Pricing Analyzer, this is a conditional input.

The loss rate which calculated on an amortized cost basis 1 year from the reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for the instrument annualized loss rate.

The off balance sheet credit exposure as a percentage of the unfunded commitment at the reporting date. For revolving lines the undrawn at future dates includes the principal amortization. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for line of credit. The CCF value in instrument reference is used as an overlay when an unconditioned CCF is not provided and when the Off Balance Sheet EAD source is Instrument CCF. When the source is CCF, CCF in Instrument Risk Metric is the CCF value. In addition, the CCF in instrument reference is required for either Off Balance Sheet EAD source for use in Moody's models that apply a weighted average.
The expected credit loss 1 year from the reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The usage given default (UGD) value should reflect the fraction of the total commitment that is expected to be outstanding (used) in the event of default. UGD captures the EAD for the whole line unlike the credit conversion factor (CCF), which captures exposure at default (EAD) only for the undrawn. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. The UGD value in instrument reference is used as an overlay when an unconditioned UGD is needed and when the Off Balance Sheet EAD source is Instrument UGD. When the source is UGD, UGD in Instrument Risk Metric is the UGD value. In addition, the UGD in instrument reference is required for either Off Balance Sheet EAD source for use in Moody's models that apply a weighted average.
The linear scalar multiplier for the custom model data. This value is applied to the prepayment rate specified in the uploaded model data files. Valid values are 0 or greater.
The annualized prepayment rate. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

Indicates which recovery model to use for the calculation. Valid values are Post-Default Price and Ultimate Recovery. The default value is Ultimate Recovery because the assumption is that majority of the portfolio are loans.

The impairment type of the instrument in accordance with the IFRS accounting standard. If provided, this value overrides the calculated value. Valid values are: Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3. Marked as conditional to be considered for stage allocation for IFRS9.

Provides an explanation for why the stage was overridden. Possible values are the reason for the override and/or who applied the override. Marked as conditional as this input is required if the Impairment Type IFRS is provided.
The issuer's rating at the time of origination to use for stage allocation for IFRS9. When marked as conditional, it is an input to be considered for the Rating-Based Stage Allocation.
The current issuer's rating to use for stage allocation for IFRS9. When marked as conditional, it is an input to be considered for the Rating-Based Stage Allocation.

The annualized probability of default at origination. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Marked as conditional to be considered for the PD-Based Stage Allocation for IFRS9. For Capital Risk Analyzer, provide either this or PD One Year for the EBA analysis.

The delinquency status of the instrument. Valid values are Current, 30 days past due, 60 days past due, 90 days past due, 120 days past due, 150 days past due, and 180 days past due. If a value is not imported, the ImpairmentStudio application will set this value to Current. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for disclosure reports. The models may use the value in this field to indicated default. Please refer to the ImpairmentStudio Data Integration Guide for more information.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet Expected Credit Loss Amount Lifetime
Description: The expected credit loss amount for the off balance sheet item for the life of the instrument from the quantitative calculation. Expressed in reporting currency.
Indicates if the instrument is credit impaired. TRUE indicates the instrument is Credit Impaired. If a value is not imported, ImpairmentStudio assume the value is FALSE.

Indicates if the GCorr recovery parameters are those for the firm or the Global Median. TRUE indicates the parameters are for the firm. FALSE indicates the Global Median parameters. Default is FALSE. When marked as conditional, this is required to be set to TRUE if the firm recovery parameters should be calculated.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet Expected Credit Loss Amount at Maturity
Description: The expected credit loss amount for the maturity risk of the off balance sheet item. This amount is included in the expected credit loss amount of the off balance sheet item.
LGD variance parameter. Valid values are between 1.1 and 1000000. If a value is not provided, this value is set to 4 in the calculation. This is in an input for GCorr.

GCorr factor name that is in the factor group geography. This includes factor codes that have the factor type of CMA, country, EM, MSA, and state. If this value is used for the calculation of the scenario conditioned PD and LGD, its weight is 100%.

GCorr factor name that is in the factor group sector. This includes factor codes that have the factor type of industry, product, and property. If this value is used for the calculation of the scenario conditioned PD and LGD, its weight is 100%.
This value is used in the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter model to determine if the industry is a public corporate or public financial.
For reinsurance receivables, set the value to either INSURANCE - LIFE or INSURANCE - PROP/CAS/HEALTH.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet Expected Credit Loss Lifetime
Description:
The expected credit loss for the off balance sheet item for the life of the instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
ImpairmentStudio does not produce a value for this field found in instrument result.

Describes the purpose of the instrument. Valid values are: Purchase, Rate/Term Refinance, Cash Out Refinance, Debt Consolidation, Home Improvement, Other Refinance, Education, Medical, Other, and Unknown.
Indicates the securities and exchange commission (SEC) segment for reporting.

Attribute Display Name: FFIEC Code Schedule RC Amortized Cost
Description:
The FFIEC code for the amortized cost reported in schedule RC. Examples of the values for this field are: U.S. Treasury Securities, 1., or 1. U.S. Treasury securities.
For the disclosure reports, use FFIEC Code Schedule RC-C, FFIEC Code Schedule RC-C Level 2, or FFIEC Code Schedule RC-C Level 3.

Attribute Display Name: FFIEC Code Schedule RI-C Allowance Balance
Description:
The FFIEC code for the allowance reported in schedule RI-C. Examples of the values for this field are: Real estate loans: Construction loans, 1.a., or 1.a.1. Real estate loans: Construction loans.
For the disclosure reports, use FFIEC Code Schedule RI-C or FFIEC Code Schedule RI-C Level 2.

Forecast variables are user defined line items at the FR Y-16 or one level lower based on the configuration and enable values to be provided for each CRA forecast item in the reports to define the calculation framework. Clients can provide these as part of Customized forecast upload to add management adjustments, additional data (Broker-dealer income) or provide reporting date reconciliation amounts.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet Expected Credit Loss Amount One Year
Description:
The expected credit loss amount for the off balance sheet item 1 year from the reporting date.
ImpairmentStudio does not produce a value for this field found in instrument result.
Indicates the allowance segment for reporting.
The weight applied to the point-in-time (PIT) probability of default (PD). Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

Floor Scenario Expected Credit Loss (ECL), enables you to indicate which ECL is floored to zero. When the attribute's value is set to TRUE, the expected loss is floored for all scenarios. This value is equivalent to the analysis settings Expected Credit Loss Zero Floor option Scenario-based Expected Credit Loss. When the value is set to FALSE or it is not provided, the expected credit loss is floored for the Summary scenario. This is equivalent to the analysis settings option Summary Expected Credit Loss. This applies to the DCF: Expected Cash Flow loss estimation methodology.

TRUE indicates that for the instrument risk metric term structure, the first period is adjusted when the instrument risk metric reporting date is not equal to the analysis reporting date. This attribute also contains the value from the analysis settings Quarter End Adjustment option. When Adjust First Period Value is selected, this is set to TRUE. When Shift to Quarter End is selected, this is set to FALSE. When no value is provided, the default is FALSE.

The real estate forecast defines the forecast package (vintage). Spring: March vintage, Summer: June vintage, Fall: Sept vintage, Winter: Dec vintage. The forecast package has a Qx 20xx which indicates the end of historical data. Valid values are quarter end dates. For example 2020-12-31. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

Attribute Display Name: Off Balance Sheet Expected Credit Loss One Year
Description: The expected credit loss for the off balance sheet item 1 year from the reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The source of the Moody's long term rating for the PD (longTermRatingForPD).
The principal loss amount remaining.
The source of the Moody's long term rating for LGD (longTermRatingForLGD).
The amount of the interest loss remaining.

The ticker of the obligor/assuming entity. An abbreviation used to uniquely identify publicly traded shares of a particular stock on a particular stock market. The ticker may consist of letters, numbers or a combination of both.
The next date on which the offered security may be called. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.
The Moody's instrument identifier.
The amount of the loss that is not due to credit.
The Moody's organization identifier.
TRUE indicates that the instrument has the minimum inputs to run the RiskCalc LGD model.
TRUE indicates that the instrument has the minimum inputs to for the MIS Structured Finance PD model.
Custom dimension.
Custom dimension.
The annualized loss rate which is calculated on an amortized cost basis based on the term to maturity. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1

This field is repurposed for the retail lender type.
For Auto Portfolio Analyzer, the type of lender providing the auto loan. Valid values are Bank, Captive, and CreditUnion.
For Portfolio Analyzer Student Loan, the type of lender providing the student loan. Valid values are Federal, FFLEP, and Private.
Ratio of monthly auto loan payment to monthly income. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are greater than 0 to 1.
TRUE indicates that the loan has a co-borrower. If no value is provided, FALSE is assumed.
The vehicle identification number which is a 16 character string. Marked as conditional as this is a required input when the Vehicle Type is not provided.
The vehicle type. Valid values are Car, Truck, SUV, Motorcycle, and RV. Marked as conditional as this is required when the VIN is not provided.
The bucket into which the lifetime annualized loss rate falls.
TRUE indicates that the vehicle is used.
The age in months of the used vehicle at the time of purchase.
The number of securities held. The close price multiplied by the number of securities equals the fair value.
Indicates when the Commercial Mortgage Metric model starts its forecast. The valid dates are the quarter end dates. For example, 2020-12-31. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

The date used to calculate the effective interest rate at origination. This is either the origination or acquisition date. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Marked as conditional as this is a required input to calculate the effective interest rate (EIR) at origination.

The loss rate which is calculated on an amortized cost basis based on the term to maturity. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
For ImpairmentStudio, this is marked as conditional as this is a required input for the instrument lifetime loss rate and for the scenario based lifetime loss rate.
For Relationship Pricing Analyzer, this is a conditional input.

The gross carrying amount at the time of origination or acquisiton. This amount is expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is a required input to calculate the effective interest rate (EIR) at origination.

For IFRS, this equals the gross carrying amount. For CECL, this equals the amortized cost. This amount is calculated at origination or acquisition date. This amount is expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is a required input to calculate the effective interest rate (EIR) at origination.

The outstanding contractual principal balance on the origination or aqcuisition date. The amount is expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional as this is a required input to calculate the effective interest rate (EIR) at origination.
For ImpairmentStudio, the initial unpaid principal balance is expected to be a positive value.
Custom dimension.
Custom dimension.
The bucket into which the lifetime cumulative loss rate falls.

Custom dimension.
Custom dimension.
Custom dimension.
Custom number.
The annualized probability of default derived from qualitative scorecards. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
Custom number.

The full outstanding contractual principal balance at loan level on the reporting date. For a Line of Credit, this is the used/drawn amount. For defaulted instruments, this amount is as of the default date. The amount is expressed in instrument currency.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input to use ownership portion to calculate the allowance at position level.
For ImpairmentStudio, the full unpaid principal balance is expected to be a positive value.

The full current commitment amount of the line of credit expressed in instrument currency at facility level. Valid values are greater than 0. Marked as conditional as this is required if the instrument type is Line of Credit.
The annualized probability of default for the life of the instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

This is the amount of regularly scheduled payment, the level principal and interest payment from the borrower, if known. Expressed in the instrument currency.
For student loans, the fixed payment for student loan applies when the student payment loan type is Fixed Payment in school. The payment is made when student is still in school and it will be applied to payoff accrued interest first before applied to principal.

The full cumulative credit amount drawn from the origination date to the as of date of a non-revolving line of credit expressed in instrument currency. Valid values are 0 or greater. When marked as conditional, this is an input to calculate exposure at facility level for instrument type Line of Credit.

The unused portion of lines of credit at loan level or the unfunded balance on a loan commitment at loan level expressed in instrument currency. Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for the instrument type Line of Credit position level calculation if the full unpaid principal balance is not provided. It is recommended to provide full unpaid principal balance.

The total assets of the borrower which is the sum of the current assets, plus net property, plant, and equipment, plus other non-current assets (including intangible assets, deferred items, and investments and advances). The amount is in local currency. For instrument categories, the local currency is the value in instrument currency.
Total sales of the borrower. The amount is in local currency. For instrument categories, the local currency is the value in instrument currency.
The bucket into which the lifetime annualized PD falls.

Attribute Display Name: On Balance Sheet EAD Scaling Factor
Description: The linear scalar multiplier for the custom model data. This value is applied to all EAD values specified in the uploaded model data files.

A value of TRUE indicates allowance amount (lossAllowanceDelta, ifrsAlowanceAmountDelta) is floored to 0 to avoid negative amounts. If the value is FALSE or no value is imported, flooring does not apply.

The asset R-squared to use when it cannot be source from the model data provided in the application. Valid values are between 0.0001 and 1.
Please work with your Moody's Analytics representative to supply this value.

The recovery R-squared value to use when it cannot be source from the model data provided in the application. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Please work with your Moody's Analytics representative to supply this value.

Correlation between the asset and recovery processes for an instrument to use when it cannot be source from the model data provided in the application. Valid values are between 0 and 1. Please work with your Moody's Analytics representative to supply this value.
The annualized probability of default 1 year from the reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

Attribute Display Name: Primary GCorr Factor Name Sector Preset
Description: GCorr factor name to use when it cannot be source from the model data provided in the application.
The sub version of the model used to calculate the PD.
The sub version of the model used to calculate the LGD.
The sub version of the model used to calculate the prepayment.

The debt to income ratio. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Marked as conditional as it is a required input for Mortgage Portfolio Analyzer for the Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae 2020 models.
The bucket into which the one year annualized PD falls.
The make of the vehicle. See list of valid values.
Ultimate cure rate for the loan, i.e. the probability that a loan, after it reaches 90DPD, will never default. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

Instruments with this field populated indicates that it meets underwriting guidelines set by the agency. Valid values are Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation and Federal National Mortgage Association.
The adjusted capitalized cost. This is the negotiated price minus the down payment. Expressed in instrument currency.
The down payment on the vehicle. Expressed in instrument currency.
The cumulative probability of default for the life of the instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The contractual residual value at the end of the lease term. Expressed in instrument currency.
Estimated number of miles driven by the vehicle in a year.
Exterior color of vehicle. Valid values are: BLACK, BLUE, BROWN, GOLD, GRAY, GREEN, ORANGE, PURPLE, RED, WHITE, and N/A. If the value is not provided, the color is assumed to be unknown.
Interior color of vehicle. Valid values are: BLACK, BLUE, GRAY, RED, TAN, WHITE, and N/A. If the value is not provided, the color is assumed to be unknown.
The method by which the vehicle is likely to be sold. Valid values are: As Is, Dealer, Fleet/Lease, Manufacturer/Factory, Repossession, Salvage, and N/A. If blank, N/A is assumed.
The bucket into which the lifetime cumulative PD falls.
Value used to stress the return rate level predicted by the model. Valid values are positive numbers.

The asset class used for benchmarking. Valid values are: Commercial and industrial, Commercial real estate, Retail, Securities, and Other.
Marked as conditional as this is a required for Data Alliance.

The asset sub class used for benchmarking. Valid values are: Construction loans, Commercial real estate loans, Residential real estate loans, Credit cards, and Other consumer loans.
Marked as conditional as this is a required for Data Alliance.

The NCUA code reported in Form 5300 Loans & Leases. Examples of the values for this field are: 1_Unsecured Credit Card Loans, 2_Payday Alternative Loans (PALs I and PALs II) (FCU Only), 3_Non-Federally Guaranteed Student Loans, 4_All Other Unsecured Loans/Lines of Credit - include SBA PPP loans, 5_New Vehicle Loans, etc.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports.

The FFIEC segment code reported in schedule RC-C. Examples of the values for this field are: 1. Loans secured by real estate, 2. Loans to depository institutions and acceptances of other banks, 3. Loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers, 4. Commercial and industrial loans, etc.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports.

The FFIEC sub-segment code reported in schedule RC-C. Examples of the values for this field are: a. Construction, land development, and other land loans, b. Secured by farmland, c. Secured by 1-4 family residential properties, etc.

The FFIEC sub-segment code reported in schedule RC-C. Examples of the values for this field are: 1. 1-4 family residential construction loans, 2. Other construction loans and all land development and other land loans, etc.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports.

The FFIEC sub-segment code reported in schedule RC-C. Examples of the values for this field are: a. Secured by first liens, b. Secured by junior liens.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports.

The FFIEC segment code reported in schedule RI-B. Examples of the values for this field are: 1. Loans secured by real estate, 4. Commercial and industrial loans, 5. Loans to individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures, etc?
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports.

The FFIEC sub-segment code reported in schedule RI-B. Examples of the values for this field are: a. Construction, land development, and other land loans, b. Secured by farmland, c. Secured by 1-4 family residential properties, etc.

The FFIEC sub-segment code reported in schedule RI-B. Examples of the values for this field are: 1. 1-4 family residential construction loans, 2. Other construction loans and all land development and other land loans, etc.

The FFIEC sub-segment code reported in schedule RI-B. Examples of the values for this field are: a. Secured by first liens, b. Secured by junior liens.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports.

The FFIEC segment code reported in schedule RI-C. Examples of the values for this field are: 1. Real estate loans, 2. Commercial loans, 3. Credit cards, etc?

When marked as conditional, this is a required input for disclosure reports.

Indicates if whether the lessor is a Capitve or a Bank. If no value is provided, Captive is assumed. Non-captive institutions such as credit unions should be coded as Bank.
User provided allowance expressed in instrument currency. If provided, this will override any calculated allowance.
The user provided expected credit losses for the on balance sheet credit exposures. Expressed in instrument currency. If provided, this value will override the calculated on balance sheet reserve.

TRUE indicates that the prepayment rate is a curtailment rate. FALSE or null indicates that the prepayment rate is a prepayment rate.

TRUE indicates that the instrument is currently in probation period. For IFRS9.

The impairment type from the prior period. Valid values are: Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3. For IFRS9. Used for the Stage Allocation probation period feature.

Otherwise, the incurred loss rate is calculated from the TTC Annualized PD One Year and TTC Annualized LGD One Year. The other use of this value is for when an unconditional LGD is required but the LGD model selected produces conditional LGDs. For the result, this value is the LGD used to calculate the one year expected credit loss amount.

The long run term specifies the time at which the long run loss rate applies for the loss rate term structures. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0.5 or greater. Valid values are 0.5 or greater and the value must be one quarter (0.25) greater than the Loss Rate Reasonable and Supportable Term.

The expected loss (EL) pertaining to the refinance risk for the instrument. Maturity measures are given for instruments maturing in the next 10 years. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The loss given default (LGD) pertaining to the refinance risk for the instrument. Maturity measures are given for instruments maturing in the next 10 years. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The loss rate which is annualized and calculated on an amortized cost basis or balance based on the term defined for this value. For the period when the instrument's time to maturity is less than one year, this value is the same as its lifetime loss rate. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. This is a required input for the annualized loss rate from a loss rate model and for a loss rate curve. Marked as conditional as this is a required input for the scenario based annualized loss rate.
The cumulative loss rate. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. This corresponds to the lifetime loss rate at the year to maturity.

The exposure at default which is the expected exposure given default as a proportion the reporting date unpaid principal balance. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are 0 or greater. Required when EAD source is EAD factor.

The off balance sheet credit exposure as a percentage of the unfunded commitment at the reporting date. For revolving lines the undrawn at future dates includes the principal amortization. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for line of credit. The CCF value in instrument reference is used as an overlay when an unconditioned CCF is not provided and when the Off Balance Sheet EAD source is Instrument CCF. When the source is CCF, CCF in Instrument Risk Metric is the CCF value. In addition, the CCF in instrument reference is required for either Off Balance Sheet EAD source for use in Moody's models that apply a weighted average.

Attribute Display Name: On Balance Sheet Loss Rate Lifetime Adjusted
Description: The on-balance sheet reserve rate after applying adjustments. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The usage given default (UGD) value should reflect the fraction of the total commitment that is expected to be outstanding (used) in the event of default. UGD captures the EAD for the whole line unlike the credit conversion factor (CCF), which captures exposure at default (EAD) only for the undrawn. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. The UGD value in instrument reference is used as an overlay when an unconditioned UGD is needed and when the Off Balance Sheet EAD source is Instrument UGD. When the source is UGD, UGD in Instrument Risk Metric is the UGD value. In addition, the UGD in instrument reference is required for either Off Balance Sheet EAD source for use in Moody's models that apply a weighted average.
The annualized cumulative prepayment rate. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The prepayment amount of each period as a percentage of the unpaid principal balance after amortization. It's annualized rate expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The cumulative prepayment rate. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The fraction of the principal expected to be recovered. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The charge off net of recovery defined as a fraction of the principal that is defaulted. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

ImpairmentStudio calculates the vintage of the instrument from the origination date. Its format is as yyyyQ#, where # is 1, 2, 3, or 4. For the MA Expected Consumer Credit Loss models, vintages prior to 2005 does not adhere to the ImpairmentStudio calculated vintage. For vintages prior to 2005, you must import these vintage values: 1990Q3 for instruments originated prior to 1990, 1995Q3 for origination between 1990 to 1995, and the year + Q3 for 1996 to 2004 (i.e. 1996Q3, 2004Q3).

The loss given default as a percentage of the exposure. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are equal to or greater than 0. Valid values for the input LGD into the GCorr model are between 0 and 1.

In instrumentReference, this is the remaining exposure (outstanding balance or for lines of credit, the current commitment amount) expressed in instrument currency. For the MA Expected Consumer Credit Loss models, this is the value for the cohort and expressed in USD.
In instrument risk metric, this amount is the relevant balance value to use for calculating rates for Capital Risk Analyzer.

The expected credit loss for the life of the instrument. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
ImpairmentStudio does not produce a value for this field found in instrument result.

An identifier to uniquely identify each entity. Each entity must have one entity identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the entity identifier for any other entity. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information.
The Moody's Analytics counterparty identifier.

The obligated party (bond issuer, borrower, debtor, insurer, etc.) who has an obligation to repay a debt, make a payment, do something, or to refrain from doing something, under the terms of an agreement. For reinsurance receivable, this value is the reinsurer or assuming entity's identifier. Identifiers in ImpairmentStudio should not include any nonpublic, personally identifiable information. Marked as conditional as this input is required for reinsurance receivables and the value is the reinsurer or assuming entity's identifier.
The identifier of the customer information file.

Type that describes the entity. Valid values include Private Firm, Public Firm, Sovereign, Municipality, Supranational, and SME. Unknown is used when the entity type is not known.
Marked as conditional to indicate that this is a required input for RiskCalc LGD and to create the scope for determining which Moody's analytics models to use, such as the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, MA Sovereign TTC to PIT PD Converter, and Muni Loss Forecast Model.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) 2017 primary industry for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity.
When marked as conditional, this a required input to lookup the Moody's sector (i.e. GCorr sector) for use in models such as the GCorr, MA CI Proxy PD/LGD and Loss Rate models, MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter, and RiskCalc LGD.

The Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) is a four-digit code for the primary industry for the obligor/assuming entity's economic activity.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input to lookup the Moody's sector (i.e. GCorr sector).

The industry or sector recognized by Moody's models. Valid values are Agriculture, ArtsAndEntertainment, Auto, Business_Products, Business_Services, Communication, Construction, Consumer_Products, Finance, Education, Health_Care, HiTech, Manufacturing, Mining, Other, Other_Services, Services, Trade, Transportation, Unassigned, and Utilities.
The values listed support the following models: MA CI Loss Rate, MA CI Proxy PD/LGD, and MA Private Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter. For each model, it is a required input and each model uses a subset of the values listed above. The ImpairmentStudio Data Integration Guide provides the sector values that each model supports.
If this field is set to a Moody's industry sector value that is not supported by the specific Moody's model, the model use the value Unasssigned.

The BvD ID number is a company identifier that uniquely identifies each company included in at least one Bureau van Dijk product. The first two characters correspond to the ISO country code (according to the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 standard) where the company is incorporated. The rest of a BvD ID number may vary and be based on a national ID, an information provider's ID, an own internal ID. As a result, BvD ID numbers may be subject to change due to changes in national IDs or an information provider's ID. For ImpairmentStudio, marked as a conditional input as this is a required for the entity lookup for the RiskCalc PD model.

The identifier of the financial statement from which the total assets and total sales were taken. A valid value for this reference is the financial statement identifier or if unique, the financial statement date.

The total assets of the borrower which is the sum of the current assets, plus net property, plant, and equipment, plus other non-current assets (including intangible assets, deferred items, and investments and advances). The amount entered in instrument or entity reference must be in USD. The amount in the financial statement is in the currency for the financial statement. The amount in instrument reference is a required input for the MA CI Proxy PD/LGD models as it is used to calculate the Total Assets Size Bucket. When marked as conditional, the amount in instrument reference is a required input to lookup the GCorr R-squared parameters for private financial firms.

Total sales of the borrower. The amount entered in instrument or entity reference must be in USD. The amount in the financial statement is in the currency for the financial statement. When marked as conditional, the value in instrument reference is a required input to lookup the GCorr R-squared parameters for private non financial firms.

The country of the headquarters for the entity's (borrower's) or its incorporation country where the obligor/assuming entity has a legal representation. For an entity that is a branch, this will be the country of the parent entity. Valid values are the 3 letter country codes from ISO (https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html). This is an input for the PD converter models such as the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter and MA Sovereign TTC to PIT PD Converter.
For entity financial statement, this is a required field.
For Loan Monitoring, in entity financial statement, this field will be set to USA.
The entity's (borrower's) incorporation state or province where the obligor/assuming entity has a legal representation.
The entity's (borrower's) incorporation zip code.
The internal rating.

Attribute Display Name: Moody's Long Term Rating for PD
Description:
The Moody's long term rating.
For the following use cases, it is the issuer rating.

This rating is used for PD conversion. Either provide this or TTC Annualized PD One Year for the MA Public Firm TTC to PIT PD Converter.

For the MA Private Firm TTC Rating to TTC PD mapping, this is the input rating.

For Capital Risk Analyzer, provide either this or PD One Year for the EBA analysis.
For structured product instruments, this is the current tranche rating.
When marked as conditional, this is a required input for the disclosure reports.
The date that the rating was issued. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

The model name used to generate the point-in-time (PIT) probability of default (PD) curve. Used with private firm entities only. Valid values are: USA 4.0, UDS 4.0, UNP 4.0, URO 4.0, USF 4.0, LNA 3.1, AUS 4.0, AUT 3.2, BEL 4.0, BNK 4.0, CAN 3.2, CHE 3.1, CHN 3.1, DEU 3.2, DNK 4.0, EMM 3.1, ESP 4.0, FIN 4.0, FRA 4.0, GBR 4.0, GRC 4.0, ITA 4.0, JPN 3.2, LFE 4.0, NLD 4.0, NOR 4.0, PRT 4.0, RUS 3.1, SGP 4.0, SWE 4.0, ZAF 3.2, ILH 3.1, and IPC 3.1.

Conditional forward PD value used for mean reversion. This is the asymptotic PD used as the mean-reversion level for conditional forward PD. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

PD term used for mean reversion. This value is beyond the at least one quarter beyond the reasonable and supportable term. The long run term specifies the time at which the instrument will tend to its long run average rate. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0.5 or greater and the value must be one quarter (0.25) greater than the PD Reasonable and Supportable Term.
The linear scalar multiplier for the custom model data. This value is applied to all PD values specified in the uploaded model data files.

The annualized probability of default derived from quantitative models or mapped from the rating produced in the scorecard. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.
The annualized probability of default derived from qualitative scorecards. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

The annualized probability of default implied from the master rating scale . Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

Legal Entity Identifier, is a unique identifier for persons that are legal entities or structures including companies, charities and trusts. The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-character, alpha-numeric code, to uniquely identify legally distinct entities that engage in financial transactions.

The unconditional point in time probability of default (PD) 1 year from reporting date. The credit cycle adjusted PD. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1.

If provided along with the LGD one year, these are used to calculate the incurred loss rate for the loss estimation method is PD/LGD. Otherwise, the incurred loss rate is calculated from the TTC Annualized PD One Year and TTC Annualized LGD One Year.

The final rating for the company. For CreditLens, the rating is that for final PD. For the scorecard product, the final rating is one of these ratings standalone, post parent support, or post government support. In addition, the final rating may be the rating after all overrides and adjustments have been made.

The one-year through-the-cycle (TTC) probability of default (PD) for the company. For CreditLens, the PD can be the quantitative, scorecard, or the rating implied PD. For the scorecard, this PD is one of these PDs, standalone, post parent support, or post government support. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.

The contractual maturity date of the instrument or the expected maturity for those instruments without a contractual maturity date. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd. Marked as conditional as this is a required input if the maturity date cannot be derived from Year to Maturity.

When the PD source is Instrument PD, this is the corresponding PD value. The loss given default value 1 year from the reporting date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1. Valid values are between 0 and 1. If provided along with the PD one year, these are used to calculate the incurred loss rate for the loss estimation method is PD/LGD. Otherwise, the incurred loss rate is calculated from the TTC Annualized PD One Year and TTC Annualized LGD One Year. The other use of this value is for when an unconditional LGD is required but the LGD model selected produces conditional LGDs. For the result, this value is the LGD used to calculate the one year expected credit loss amount.

LGD variance parameter. Valid values are between 1.1 and 1000000. If a value is not provided, this value is set to 4 in the calculation. This is in an input for GCorr.

GCorr factor name that is in the factor group geography. This includes factor codes that have the factor type of CMA, country, EM, MSA, and state. If this value is used for the calculation of the scenario conditioned PD and LGD, its weight is 100%.

The calculation method used to calculate the expected credit loss.
The methods vary by accounting classification.
For the Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL), the loss estimation methods vary by ASC impairment evaulation. For instruments that are collectively evaluated the valid values are: DCF: Expected Cash Flow, DCF: Loss Cash Flow, Loss Rate, PD/LGD, and Imported Allowance. For instruments that are individually evaluated the valid values are: Collateral Value, DCF: Expected Cash Flow, DCF: Loss Cash Flow, Loan's Fair Value, Loss Rate, PD/LGD, and Imported Allowance.
For the International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), the the valid values are: DCF: Expected Cash Flow, DCF: Loss Cash Flow, PD/LGD, and Imported Allowance.
Indicates whether or not to apply the scenario conditioning to the PD. TRUE applies scenario conditioning and FALSE does not apply scenario conditioning.

The reasonable and supportable period for forecasting the PD. This value is used for mean reversion. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0.25 and greater.

PD term used for mean reversion. This value is beyond the at least one quarter beyond the reasonable and supportable term. The long run term specifies the time at which the instrument will tend to its long run average rate. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0.5 or greater and the value must be one quarter (0.25) greater than the PD Reasonable and Supportable Term.
Indicates whether or not to apply the scenario conditioning to the LGD. TRUE applies scenario conditioning and FALSE does not apply scenario conditioning.

The reasonable and supportable period for forecasting the LGD. This value is used for mean reversion. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0.25 and greater.

LGD term used for mean reversion. This value is beyond the at least one quarter beyond the reasonable and supportable term. The long run term specifies the time at which the long run LGD applies for the LGD term structures. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0.5 or greater and the value must be one quarter (0.25) greater than the LGD Reasonable and Supportable Term.
The calculation method to calculate the loss rate. Valid values are Annualized Loss Rate, Lifetime Loss Rate, and Loss Rate Curve.

Attribute Display Name: Loss Rate Reasonable and Supportable Term
Description: The reasonable and supportable period for forecasting the loss rate. This value is used for mean reversion. Expressed as a fraction of a year in decimal format. For example, 1 year and 6 months is 1.5. Valid values are 0.25 and greater.

Loss rate term used for mean reversion. This value is beyond the at least one quarter beyond the reasonable and supportable term. None corresponds to an empty value, that is, after you provide the annuity, or the software calculates it, there is no further processing, and the amount remains the same on subsequent constant installment annuity reset dates.
Index value at the last reset date at or before Reporting Date. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
The currency of the residual value for the lease. If no value is provided, the value is assumed to be the instrument currency.

Indicates individually assessed instruments. TRUE indicates the instrument will be individually assessed. When set to TRUE, these instruments will be selected for individual assessment in Step 4 - Summary. Default values is FALSE.
GCorr factor name that is in the factor group geography, associated specifically with recovery. This includes factor codes that have the factor type of CMA, country, EM, MSA, and state.
If true, the probability of repossession is calculated and used in the LGD model under Auto Portfolio Analyzer model.
User provided probability of repossession of the vehicle in case of default under Auto Portfolio Analyzer model. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
GCorr factor name that is in the factor group sector, associated specifically with recovery. This includes factor codes that have the factor type of industry, product, and property.
The constant default rate is the forward probability of default. Expressed in decimal format. For example, 10% is 0.1.
Unique Name of template. Required field
The amount of the balance that is deferred. Expressed in the currency of the instrument.

Indicates if the record is a booked deal for the current reporting period, simulated growth, or a combination (total) of booked and simulated transactions. Valid values are Booked, Simulated, and Combined.

The identifier of the account. In the account hierarchy, this is a unique identifier.
In the account hierarchy, the Account Identifier includes the Account Side, Hierarchy 1 to Hierarchy 9, and Account Name.
The mode users select to generate Probability of Default using EDFx model. Valid values are: Financial Statement, BvD ID, PID, Entity International Name, ISIN, CUSIP, and LEI

Attribute Display Name: Rating Stage Map As of Date
Description: The version of the rating stage map used to generate the results for this instrument. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

Attribute Display Name: PD Based Template As of Date
Description: The version of the PD based template used to generate the results for this instrument. Formatted as yyyy-mm-dd.

Specifies the timing for recognizing the loss associated with the instrument with the bullet interest payment schedule.
If set to TRUE or empty, the loss is recognized periodically (e.g., quarterly).
If set to FALSE, the loss is recognized only upon maturity of the instrument.
It represents the provisions for loan and lease losses, charges against a bank’s operating income which are either likely or certain to be incurred. Unit is thousands.
